        var title_attribute_dictionary = titleStructure[2];
        var title_text_content = titleStructure[3];
        for (var title_attribute_key in title_attribute_dictionary) {
          title_element.setAttribute(title_attribute_key, title_attribute_dictionary[title_attribute_key]);
        }

        var title_text_node = document.createTextNode(title_text_content);
        title_element.appendChild(title_text_node);


        level_element.appendChild(title_element);
      }

      /* last level place links */
      if (i == overallStructure.length-1) {
        for (var link_i=0; link_i < links.length; link_i++) {
          var link = links[link_i];
          for (var linkStructureIndex=0;linkStructureIndex < linkStructure.length; linkStructureIndex++) {
            BELinkBlockGenerator.generateIndividualLinks(level_element, linkStructure[linkStructureIndex], link)
          }
        }
      }

      /* first level child we need to check placement */
      if (previousElement == targetElement) {
        if (replacementStrategy == BELinkBlockGenerator.REPLACEMENT_STRATEGY_PRE_APPEND_ELEMENT) {
          /* 2 means insert right before */
          previousElement.insertBefore(level_element, targetElement.firstChild);
        } else if (replacementStrategy == BELinkBlockGenerator.REPLACEMENT_STRATEGY_PRE_APPEND_PARENT) {
          /* 3 means insert right before at parent level */
          var parentElement = previousElement.parentElement;
          parentElement.insertBefore(level_element, previousElement);
        } else {
          previousElement.appendChild(level_element);
        }
      } else {
        previousElement.appendChild(level_element);
      }
      previousElement = level_element;
    }
  };
}(window.BELinkBlockGenerator = window.BELinkBlockGenerator || {}))
};
</script>

/* Add padding to create space for the caret on dropdown menu items on homepage */
.home .has-dropdown {
    padding-right: 28px; /* Add enough space for the caret to avoid overlap */
}

/* Adjust caret color and size, keeping the initial perfect positioning */
.home .has-dropdown:after {
    position: absolute;
    top: 0; /* Keep original vertical positioning */
    right: 10px; /* Maintain original right position */
    font-size: 11px; /* Adjust size to match your preference */
    content: "\e64b"; /* Use the Themify caret icon */
    font-family: 'themify'; /* Ensure caret uses the Themify icon font */
    color: #666666 !important; /* Change the caret color to dark */
    font-weight: normal !important; /* Ensure caret is thin */
}

/* Remove any extra custom arrows from homepage non-sticky menu */
.home .menu-item-has-children > a::after {
    content: none !important; /* Remove any extra arrows */
}


/* Ensure no extra arrows are present in the sticky or submenus */
nav.fixed .menu-item-has-children > a::after,
.menu-item-has-children > ul > li.menu-item-has-children > a::after {
    content: none !important; /* Prevent extra arrows in sticky or submenu items */
}


/* DB: Styling fix for menu-item with current ancestor/parent state */
.menu-item.menu-item-type-custom.current-menu-ancestor.current-menu-parent {
    line-height: 20px;
    margin-top: 0px !important; /* Ensure consistent margin for current ancestor/parent */
}

/* DB: Combined styling fix for specific menu items (menu-item-25441, menu-item-25443, and menu-item-25444) */
li#menu-item-25441.menu-item,
li#menu-item-25443.menu-item.menu-item-type-custom.current-menu-ancestor.current-menu-parent,
li#menu-item-25444.menu-item {
    line-height: 20px;
    margin-top: 45px !important; /* Add 45px top margin */
}

/* DB: Adjust spacing for mobile and tablet (max-width: 991px) */
@media (max-width: 991px) {
    li#menu-item-25441.menu-item,
    li#menu-item-25443.menu-item.menu-item-type-custom.current-menu-ancestor.current-menu-parent,
    li#menu-item-25444.menu-item {
        margin-top: 0px !important; /* Remove extra margin on mobile/tablet */
        line-height: normal !important; /* Set normal line height */
        padding-top: 0px !important; /* Ensure consistent top padding */
        padding-bottom: 0px !important; /* Ensure consistent bottom padding */
    }
}

/* DB: Ensure submenus remain within viewport on tablets and smaller screens (max-width: 1024px) */
@media (max-width: 1024px) {
    .menu > li > ul > li > ul {
        left: auto !important; /* Prevent submenus from going off the screen to the left */
        right: 0 !important; /* Align submenus with the right edge */
        top: 0 !important; /* Align submenu to the top of the parent */
        position: absolute !important; /* Ensure proper positioning */
    }

    /* DB: Make sure parent items with submenus remain clickable */
    .menu > li > ul > li > a {
        pointer-events: auto !important; /* Enable pointer events for clickable parent items */
    }

    /* DB: Remove custom arrow in submenu, retain default arrow */
    .menu > li > ul > li:has(ul) > a::after {
        content: none !important; /* Remove custom arrow */
    }
}

/* DB: Mobile-specific adjustments for better touch experience (max-width: 767px) */
@media (max-width: 767px) {
    .menu > li > ul > li > ul {
        position: static !important; /* Stack submenus directly under parent menu */
        width: 100% !important; /* Make submenu full width */
        right: auto !important; /* Remove right alignment */
        left: 0 !important; /* Align submenu to the left */
        margin-left: -20px !important; /* Prevent submenu from overlapping */
    }

    /* DB: Ensure parent menu items are fully clickable on mobile */
    .menu > li > ul > li > a {
        display: block;
        width: 100%; /* Make parent items take full width */
        padding: 10px 0; /* Add padding for better touch experience */
    }

    /* DB: Position default arrow closer to text in last-level dropdown */
    .menu > li > ul > li > ul > li:has(ul) > a::after {
        content: "\276F"; /* Default arrow */
        font-size: 1.2em;
        margin-left: 6px; /* Position arrow closer to text */
        display: inline-block;
        vertical-align: middle; /* Align arrow with text */
        transform: rotate(90deg); /* Rotate arrow to point downwards */
    }
}
/* DB: End styling fix */
/* DB: Ensure the top-level parent (menu-item-25441) always has a gray caret */
@media (max-width: 991px) {
    /* Target the top-level parent with the ID of menu-item-25441 */
    #menu-item-25441.has-dropdown:after {
        color: #808080!important; /* Gray caret for this top-level parent */
    }

    /* Maintain white caret for dropdowns inside the submenu (for nested dropdowns) */
    body.home nav.transparent .has-dropdown .has-dropdown:after {
        color: #ffffff!important; /* White caret for nested dropdown items */
    }
}
/* DB: White caret for all dropdowns (except uppermost parent) on homepage (desktop and sticky menu) */
@media (min-width: 992px) {
    /* White caret for child dropdowns on homepage (excluding uppermost parent) */
    body.home nav.transparent .has-dropdown .has-dropdown:after {
        color: #ffffff!important; /* White caret for all nested dropdowns */
    }
}

/* DB: Sticky menu fix for white caret (excluding uppermost parent) on homepage */
nav.sticky-menu {
    /* White caret for child dropdowns in sticky menu (excluding uppermost parent) */
    body.home nav.sticky-menu .has-dropdown .has-dropdown:after {
        color: #ffffff!important; /* White caret for all nested dropdowns in sticky menu */
    }

}
h4.mb0:empty,
h4.mb0:empty::before,
h4.mb0:empty::after {
    display: none !important;
}
/* Custom styling for <h1> on team pages */
/* Added by DB (Dan Batten) to ensure specific styles for team member names */
body.single-team h1.mb0 {
    margin-bottom: -15px !important;
    font-size: 25px;
    font-weight: 500;
}
/* ==========================================================================
   Dev Notes: Social Icons Mobile Fix, Hover Effects & Clearfix
   --------------------------------------------------------------------------
   1) Re-enable clicks on preview-only anchors
   2) Expand tap targets on touchscreens
   3) Apply hover/active effects on desktop & mobile
   4) Clear any floats so nothing overlaps on small screens
   ========================================================================== */

/* 1) Undo Customizer’s pointer-events:none lock */
.customize-unpreviewable {
  pointer-events: auto !important;
}

/* 2) Improve tap targets by making links inline-block with padding */
ul.list-inline.social-list li a {
  display: inline-block;    /* allows padding/width on the <a> */
  padding: 0px;             /* increase hit area for touch */
  margin: 0 0px;            /* spacing between icons */
  line-height: 1;           /* vertically center the icon */
}

/* 3) Trigger hover/active effects for mouse & touch users */
ul.list-inline.social-list li a:hover i,
ul.list-inline.social-list li a:active i,
ul.list-inline.social-list li a:focus i {
  color: #ffffff;                  /* change icon color on interaction */
  transform: scale(1.1);           /* slightly enlarge icon */
  transition: transform 0.2s ease; /* smooth scaling animation */
}

/* 4) Clear any floated elements that come after the social list */
.social-list-clear {
  clear: both;   /* forces following content below any floats */
  width: 100%;   /* spans full container (optional) */
  height: 0;     /* no visual height—purely a clearfix */
}
/* Desktop styling for main team member names on the team page */
@media (min-width: 1025px) {
    .about-our-team h2.uppercase,
    body.page-id-56 h2.uppercase { /* Replace XXXX with the page ID of /about/our-team/ */
        font-size: 1.5em!important;
        line-height: 1.5em!important;
    }
}
@media (min-width: 1025px) {
  body.home .image-tile .title h2.uppercase {
    font-size: 1.5em;
    line-height: 1.5em;
  }
}
body.postid-50657 .type-post .post-content h1 {
  display: none !important;
}

	<section class="  light-wrapper pt64 pb64  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper pt64 pb64 vc_custom_1580315170002"><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><p style="font-size: 17px;line-height: 2;text-align: center;font-family:Cardo;font-weight:400;font-style:italic" class="vc_custom_heading" >People are primarily relational beings. We do best in life when we have others we can rely on and talk to. Love, care, attention, and intention matter a great deal to us as a whole. Understanding that, at Cliffside Malibu, we look for ways to motivate people to do better, to live the lives they want instead of being trapped in addiction. Addiction is a behavior that suppresses pain. We use various sorts of therapies to help addicts express their pain in healthy ways, using the support of family, friends, and professionals, as well as other tools. DBT is one such tool.</p></div></div></div></div></div></section><div class="row light-wrapper  light-wrapper pt0 pb0   light-wrapper pt0 pb0 "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<section class="image-square left">
			    <div class="col-md-6 image">
			        <div class="background-image-holder">
			            <img decoding="async" width="1200" height="800" src="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/place-holder-girl-wrapping-legs.jpg" class="background-image" alt="Co-Occurring Disorder Treatment" srcset="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/place-holder-girl-wrapping-legs.jpg 1200w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/place-holder-girl-wrapping-legs-300x200.jpg 300w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/place-holder-girl-wrapping-legs-1024x683.jpg 1024w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/place-holder-girl-wrapping-legs-768x512.jpg 768w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/place-holder-girl-wrapping-legs-600x400.jpg 600w" sizes="(max-width: 1200px) 100vw, 1200px" />
			        </div>
			    </div>
			    <div class="col-md-6 col-md-offset-1 content">
			        <h2 style="font-size: 20px;color: #003057;line-height: 1.5;text-align: left;font-family:Tenor Sans;font-weight:400;font-style:normal" class="vc_custom_heading sub-header" >What is DBT?</h2>
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) was developed by Marsha Linehan, Ph.D., ABPP, a mental health researcher at the University of Washington. DBT is a type of <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/treatment-program/cognitive-behavioral-therapy/">cognitive-behavioral therapy</a> that combines psychological techniques with mindfulness awareness, to assist those with severe psychological disorders, particularly emotional disregulation. It is very often used at Cliffside Malibu to assist those with <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/cooccuring-disorder/">co-occurring disorders</a> (also called dual diagnosis) to regulate the out-of-control feelings that the addict previously used drugs and <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/alcohol/">alcohol</a> to control.</p>
<p>DBT has strong research backing and is particularly effective with disorders in which the client’s capacity for emotional arousal is above average. For addiction recovery, we use DBT to help clients:</p>
<ul>
<li>label the emotions that they are feeling;</li>
<li>recognize that the way they frame emotions can both limit and motivate them;</li>
<li>understand the ways in which their feelings affect themselves and others;</li>
<li>regulate their feelings and use them in ways that are freeing and inspiring;</li>
<li>deal with stress and distress.</li>
</ul>

		</div>
	</div>

			    </div>
			</section>
		</div></div></div></div><section class="  light-wrapper normal-padding  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper normal-padding vc_custom_1581010617537"><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><h2 style="font-size: 20px;color: #003057;line-height: 1.5;text-align: left;font-family:Tenor Sans;font-weight:400;font-style:normal" class="vc_custom_heading sub-header" >How DBT Helps?</h2>
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>Addicts have a tendency to become overwhelmed by their emotions. In treatment, we help addicts find tools to deal with what would otherwise be overpowering emotional states. DBT is a powerful tool in this task.</p>
<p>DBT is used in conjunction with other ideas about psychology, such as the <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/treatment-program/stages-of-change/">Stages of Change model.</a> In the Stages of Change model, people are approached at their level of readiness to change. Once a person’s current level of ability to change has been identified, DBT principles can be used.</p>
<p>The relationship between the therapist and the client when using DBT is active. In DBT, the therapist teaches tools to help the client deal with their emotions in healthier ways than they have in the past. This is a direct method of addressing happiness and providing skills to give addicts better tools for living. Traditionally in the field of psychology, therapists implied a lot of things. Sitting back in their chairs, they would vaguely suggest a course of action and hope the client figured out how to go forward for themselves. With DBT, our therapists prefer to go ahead and tell you exactly where they’re coming from and see if together you can find mutual ground, to work as a unit to improve life skills. That’s what DBT does. It allows the therapist to work with the addict to say, “Okay, your issue mostly is what you do with your emotions – when you feel these emotions you take drugs, or you cut your wrists, or you drink. We’re going to find something healthier to do with those emotions since that isn’t really working out too well.” In this way, therapist and client work together to increase the addict’s ability to connect with others and deal with emotions in meaningful ways.</p>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div></div></div></section><section class="  light-wrapper pt64 pb64  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper pt64 pb64 vc_custom_1580930862433"><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><p style="font-size: 14px;color: #ffffff;line-height: 2;text-align: center;font-family:Raleway;font-weight:400;font-style:normal" class="vc_custom_heading" ><a href="https://depts.washington.edu/uwbrtc/our-team/marsha-linehan/">For more information on Professor Linehan, visit her website at the University of Washington for resources about her research and DBT.</a></p>

		    <div class="col-sm-12 text-center"> <a class="btn btn-lg mb0 mt-xs-24" href="https://depts.washington.edu/uwbrtc/our-team/marsha-linehan/" target="_blank">Learn More</a>
		    </div>

Private Premium Dialectical Behavior Therapy in Malibu, CA 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 
 CALL FOR A CONFIDENTIAL CONSULTATION 
 Call 
 424.320.3061 
 
 

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 
 

 
 

 
 Treatment Programs

 Detox

 The Stages of Change

 About 
 
 
 Our Approach 

 
 Our Team

 Tour 

 
 Brochure 

 
 What We Treat

 Contact 
 
 
 Admissions 

 

 Careers 

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT)
 

 

 

 

 
 Home 

 Treatment Programs 

Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT)
 
 

 

 
 

People are primarily relational beings. We do best in life when we have others we can rely on and talk to. Love, care, attention, and intention matter a great deal to us as a whole. Understanding that, at Cliffside Malibu, we look for ways to motivate people to do better, to live the lives they want instead of being trapped in addiction. Addiction is a behavior that suppresses pain. We use various sorts of therapies to help addicts express their pain in healthy ways, using the support of family, friends, and professionals, as well as other tools. DBT is one such tool.

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
What is DBT?

 

 

 
Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) was developed by Marsha Linehan, Ph.D., ABPP, a mental health researcher at the University of Washington. DBT is a type of cognitive-behavioral therapy that combines psychological techniques with mindfulness awareness, to assist those with severe psychological disorders, particularly emotional disregulation. It is very often used at Cliffside Malibu to assist those with co-occurring disorders (also called dual diagnosis) to regulate the out-of-control feelings that the addict previously used drugs and alcohol to control.

DBT has strong research backing and is particularly effective with disorders in which the client’s capacity for emotional arousal is above average. For addiction recovery, we use DBT to help clients:

 

label the emotions that they are feeling;

recognize that the way they frame emotions can both limit and motivate them;

understand the ways in which their feelings affect themselves and others;

regulate their feelings and use them in ways that are freeing and inspiring;

deal with stress and distress.

 

 

 

 

 

 

How DBT Helps?

 

 

 
Addicts have a tendency to become overwhelmed by their emotions. In treatment, we help addicts find tools to deal with what would otherwise be overpowering emotional states. DBT is a powerful tool in this task.

DBT is used in conjunction with other ideas about psychology, such as the Stages of Change model. In the Stages of Change model, people are approached at their level of readiness to change. Once a person’s current level of ability to change has been identified, DBT principles can be used.

The relationship between the therapist and the client when using DBT is active. In DBT, the therapist teaches tools to help the client deal with their emotions in healthier ways than they have in the past. This is a direct method of addressing happiness and providing skills to give addicts better tools for living. Traditionally in the field of psychology, therapists implied a lot of things. Sitting back in their chairs, they would vaguely suggest a course of action and hope the client figured out how to go forward for themselves. With DBT, our therapists prefer to go ahead and tell you exactly where they’re coming from and see if together you can find mutual ground, to work as a unit to improve life skills. That’s what DBT does. It allows the therapist to work with the addict to say, “Okay, your issue mostly is what you do with your emotions – when you feel these emotions you take drugs, or you cut your wrists, or you drink. We’re going to find something healthier to do with those emotions since that isn’t really working out too well.” In this way, therapist and client work together to increase the addict’s ability to connect with others and deal with emotions in meaningful ways.

 

 

 For more information on Professor Linehan, visit her website at the University of Washington for resources about her research and DBT. 

 

 
 Learn More 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
THE BEST CALL YOU WILL EVER MAKE

Call now to speak confidentially with an admission counselor.

      for (var link_attribute_key in link_attribute_dictionary) {

/* Add padding to create space for the caret on dropdown menu items on homepage */
.home .has-dropdown {
    padding-right: 28px; /* Add enough space for the caret to avoid overlap */
}

/* Adjust caret color and size, keeping the initial perfect positioning */
.home .has-dropdown:after {
    position: absolute;
    top: 0; /* Keep original vertical positioning */
    right: 10px; /* Maintain original right position */
    font-size: 11px; /* Adjust size to match your preference */
    content: "\e64b"; /* Use the Themify caret icon */
    font-family: 'themify'; /* Ensure caret uses the Themify icon font */
    color: #666666 !important; /* Change the caret color to dark */
    font-weight: normal !important; /* Ensure caret is thin */
}

/* Remove any extra custom arrows from homepage non-sticky menu */
.home .menu-item-has-children > a::after {
    content: none !important; /* Remove any extra arrows */
}


/* Ensure no extra arrows are present in the sticky or submenus */
nav.fixed .menu-item-has-children > a::after,
.menu-item-has-children > ul > li.menu-item-has-children > a::after {
    content: none !important; /* Prevent extra arrows in sticky or submenu items */
}


/* DB: Styling fix for menu-item with current ancestor/parent state */
.menu-item.menu-item-type-custom.current-menu-ancestor.current-menu-parent {
    line-height: 20px;
    margin-top: 0px !important; /* Ensure consistent margin for current ancestor/parent */
}

/* DB: Combined styling fix for specific menu items (menu-item-25441, menu-item-25443, and menu-item-25444) */
li#menu-item-25441.menu-item,
li#menu-item-25443.menu-item.menu-item-type-custom.current-menu-ancestor.current-menu-parent,
li#menu-item-25444.menu-item {
    line-height: 20px;
    margin-top: 45px !important; /* Add 45px top margin */
}

/* DB: Adjust spacing for mobile and tablet (max-width: 991px) */
@media (max-width: 991px) {
    li#menu-item-25441.menu-item,
    li#menu-item-25443.menu-item.menu-item-type-custom.current-menu-ancestor.current-menu-parent,
    li#menu-item-25444.menu-item {
        margin-top: 0px !important; /* Remove extra margin on mobile/tablet */
        line-height: normal !important; /* Set normal line height */
        padding-top: 0px !important; /* Ensure consistent top padding */
        padding-bottom: 0px !important; /* Ensure consistent bottom padding */
    }
}

/* DB: Ensure submenus remain within viewport on tablets and smaller screens (max-width: 1024px) */
@media (max-width: 1024px) {
    .menu > li > ul > li > ul {
        left: auto !important; /* Prevent submenus from going off the screen to the left */
        right: 0 !important; /* Align submenus with the right edge */
        top: 0 !important; /* Align submenu to the top of the parent */
        position: absolute !important; /* Ensure proper positioning */
    }

    /* DB: Make sure parent items with submenus remain clickable */
    .menu > li > ul > li > a {
        pointer-events: auto !important; /* Enable pointer events for clickable parent items */
    }

    /* DB: Remove custom arrow in submenu, retain default arrow */
    .menu > li > ul > li:has(ul) > a::after {
        content: none !important; /* Remove custom arrow */
    }
}

/* DB: Mobile-specific adjustments for better touch experience (max-width: 767px) */
@media (max-width: 767px) {
    .menu > li > ul > li > ul {
        position: static !important; /* Stack submenus directly under parent menu */
        width: 100% !important; /* Make submenu full width */
        right: auto !important; /* Remove right alignment */
        left: 0 !important; /* Align submenu to the left */
        margin-left: -20px !important; /* Prevent submenu from overlapping */
    }

    /* DB: Ensure parent menu items are fully clickable on mobile */
    .menu > li > ul > li > a {
        display: block;
        width: 100%; /* Make parent items take full width */
        padding: 10px 0; /* Add padding for better touch experience */
    }

    /* DB: Position default arrow closer to text in last-level dropdown */
    .menu > li > ul > li > ul > li:has(ul) > a::after {
        content: "\276F"; /* Default arrow */
        font-size: 1.2em;
        margin-left: 6px; /* Position arrow closer to text */
        display: inline-block;
        vertical-align: middle; /* Align arrow with text */
        transform: rotate(90deg); /* Rotate arrow to point downwards */
    }
}
/* DB: End styling fix */
/* DB: Ensure the top-level parent (menu-item-25441) always has a gray caret */
@media (max-width: 991px) {
    /* Target the top-level parent with the ID of menu-item-25441 */
    #menu-item-25441.has-dropdown:after {
        color: #808080!important; /* Gray caret for this top-level parent */
    }

    /* Maintain white caret for dropdowns inside the submenu (for nested dropdowns) */
    body.home nav.transparent .has-dropdown .has-dropdown:after {
        color: #ffffff!important; /* White caret for nested dropdown items */
    }
}
/* DB: White caret for all dropdowns (except uppermost parent) on homepage (desktop and sticky menu) */
@media (min-width: 992px) {
    /* White caret for child dropdowns on homepage (excluding uppermost parent) */
    body.home nav.transparent .has-dropdown .has-dropdown:after {
        color: #ffffff!important; /* White caret for all nested dropdowns */
    }
}

/* DB: Sticky menu fix for white caret (excluding uppermost parent) on homepage */
nav.sticky-menu {
    /* White caret for child dropdowns in sticky menu (excluding uppermost parent) */
    body.home nav.sticky-menu .has-dropdown .has-dropdown:after {
        color: #ffffff!important; /* White caret for all nested dropdowns in sticky menu */
    }

}
h4.mb0:empty,
h4.mb0:empty::before,
h4.mb0:empty::after {
    display: none !important;
}
/* Custom styling for <h1> on team pages */
/* Added by DB (Dan Batten) to ensure specific styles for team member names */
body.single-team h1.mb0 {
    margin-bottom: -15px !important;

/* ==========================================================================
   Dev Notes: Social Icons Mobile Fix, Hover Effects & Clearfix
   --------------------------------------------------------------------------
   1) Re-enable clicks on preview-only anchors
   2) Expand tap targets on touchscreens
   3) Apply hover/active effects on desktop & mobile
   4) Clear any floats so nothing overlaps on small screens
   ========================================================================== */

/* 1) Undo Customizer’s pointer-events:none lock */
.customize-unpreviewable {
  pointer-events: auto !important;
}

/* 2) Improve tap targets by making links inline-block with padding */
ul.list-inline.social-list li a {
  display: inline-block;    /* allows padding/width on the <a> */
  padding: 0px;             /* increase hit area for touch */
  margin: 0 0px;            /* spacing between icons */
  line-height: 1;           /* vertically center the icon */
}

/* 3) Trigger hover/active effects for mouse & touch users */
ul.list-inline.social-list li a:hover i,
ul.list-inline.social-list li a:active i,
ul.list-inline.social-list li a:focus i {
  color: #ffffff;                  /* change icon color on interaction */
  transform: scale(1.1);           /* slightly enlarge icon */
  transition: transform 0.2s ease; /* smooth scaling animation */
}

/* 4) Clear any floated elements that come after the social list */
.social-list-clear {
  clear: both;   /* forces following content below any floats */
  width: 100%;   /* spans full container (optional) */
  height: 0;     /* no visual height—purely a clearfix */
}
/* Desktop styling for main team member names on the team page */
@media (min-width: 1025px) {
    .about-our-team h2.uppercase,
    body.page-id-56 h2.uppercase { /* Replace XXXX with the page ID of /about/our-team/ */
        font-size: 1.5em!important;
        line-height: 1.5em!important;
    }
}
@media (min-width: 1025px) {
  body.home .image-tile .title h2.uppercase {
    font-size: 1.5em;
    line-height: 1.5em;
  }
}
body.postid-50657 .type-post .post-content h1 {
  display: none !important;
}

			<!-- Schema optimized by Schema Pro --><!-- / Schema optimized by Schema Pro --><!-- Schema optimized by Schema Pro --><script type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Service","name":"Luxury Addiction Treatment","serviceType":"Luxury Residential Treatment","image":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-hazy-oceanspray-beach-cliffs-hero-tall.jpg","width":1600,"height":2400},"provider":{"@type":"LocalBusiness","name":"Cliffside Malibu","image":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-hazy-oceanspray-beach-cliffs-hero-tall.jpg","width":1600,"height":2400},"telephone":"(424) 320-3061","priceRange":"$$ - $$$$","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"Malibu, California","addressRegion":"Los Angeles Metropolitan Area","streetAddress":"29160 Heathercliff Rd Suite 200"}},"areaServed":{"@type":"State","name":"California"},"description":"If you are resisting the idea of entering residential rehab, it may be because you do not realize that you have an alcohol or drug problem. There are a few simple tests that you can use to determine if you do. Ask yourself these questions:\r\n\r\nDo you feel that you need a drink or a pill before facing certain situations, or even before starting the day? Does your life feel incomplete without alcohol or a drug? Have your family relationships suffered because of your drinking or your dependence on a pill?\r\n\r\nIf you said yes to any of those questions, then you are addicted. It is rarely a good idea to try to recover on your own. Your chances of gaining lasting sobriety without professional assistance are minimal. Learn more about our luxury residential rehab program.\n\t\t\t    \n\t\t\t\n\t\t\n\t\n\t\t\n\t\t\tCliffside Malibu offers private luxury residential rehab in California through our many residential and outpatient locations. We are located in Malibu, California, on two acres of privately owned land. You will not have to worry about your confidentiality in our residential rehab; we will respect it in every way. We also offer you the best counselors and therapists that can be found. A few of them are especially able to understand what you are going through because they have been where you are – fighting a drug addiction of some sort. Seeing their success can help you become more determined to win your own battle.\n\n\t\t\n\t\nPersonal Help\n\t\n\t\t\n\t\t\tYou do not need to enter a residential rehab that will treat you as just a number or a medical case to be solved. At Cliffside Malibu, you can be assured that this will not happen to you! We care about each person that enters our drug rehab center, and it makes a difference.\nBecause you have a personal battle to fight against drug addiction or alcoholism, we will not try to force you into a pre-existing treatment plan. The point of residential rehab is not to rush you through a program, but to help you find your own way to achieve and maintain sobriety. For that reason, our trained counselors and therapists are eager to work with you to devise a personal treatment plan.\nEvery residential rehab plan needs to be tailored to the individual. We are one of the few rehab centers that do this at all levels of treatment. You can find therapy techniques from both the Western and Eastern hemispheres. We offer the services of an acupuncturist, an equine therapist, and opportunities for individual, family, and group therapies. Give us the chance to help you find your way to sobriety.\n\n\t\t\n\t\nAlcohol Treatment in Malibu, California\n\t\n\t\t\n\t\t\tIf you are addicted to alcohol, you need to enter a residential alcohol treatment program. It’s that simple; most people are unable to defeat this addiction without professional help. Seeking help does not indicate that you are a weak person, or that you lack willpower. It says that you are strong enough to admit that you have a problem and that you are committed to beating that problem.\nOur luxury residential rehab for alcoholism includes medically-assisted detox by our on-staff medical team, individual, group, and family therapy programs. These group functions can help you see that you are not alone in your struggle. Every client here can contribute something valuable and can possibly encourage somebody else who has not yet reached the same level of success. Give yourself the chance to succeed.\n\n\t\t\n\t\n\n\t\t\t\n\t\t\t    \n\t\t\t\n\t\tDrug Treatment\n\t\n\t\t\n\t\t\tIn our luxury residential rehab in California for drug addiction, you will take place in all three types of therapy: group, individual, and family. Each one of these therapies will yield great benefits, but family therapy is certainly one of the most important aspects of treatment here.\nAt Cliffside Malibu, we also offer detoxification help. Do not trust any residential rehab that encourages you to detox outside a facility. The withdrawal symptoms of some drugs can be dangerous and even deadly. You will need professional help to make your detox as painless as possible.\n\n\t\t\n\t\nLuxury Residential Rehab in California\n\t\n\t\t\n\t\t\tWhether you are addicted to alcohol or drugs, you deserve to be treated well during your time of recovery. At Cliffside Malibu, we are happy to give you the pampering you want in order to make your experience pleasant. At our residential rehab, you will stay in comfort unparalleled by other drug abuse treatment centers.\nFrom our private bedrooms and bathrooms to the sundecks and gardens, you will be sure to notice the luxury we offer. If you need a massage, we offer the services of a skilled masseuse. All laundry and linen services are provided.\nDuring your stay with us, you will never lack for good food. Our gourmet chef prepares healthy meals to help your body recover. Don’t confuse healthy with tasteless, however! Our dinners are always delicious, and you can even request that your meals be made according to special diet requirements. This is what we think of as luxury residential rehab. Get your treatment underway and become sober again.\n\n\t\t\n\t\nFamily Help\n\t\n\t\t\n\t\t\tYou can’t beat addiction alone. Even residential rehab, by itself, is not a guaranteed “cure” for addiction. If you are going to succeed, you need a support system behind you. We offer continuing care programs to give you that extra bit of help even after you leave our residential rehab.\nIf family ties have been strained to the point where they are practically nonexistent, you need to work to restore those relationships during residential rehab. Addiction can make you do things you would never otherwise do – and your family will understand that. Our family therapy program can help you and your family to work toward reconciliation. These meetings will be overseen by a trained therapist who can ensure a productive environment. Do not be afraid to admit your addiction or to ask your family for help.\n\n\t\t\n\t\nStages of Change for Addiction Recovery\n\t\n\t\t\n\t\t\tAt Cliffside Malibu, we base our individualized luxury residential treatment plans on the Stages of Change model. The Stages of Change model, created by James O. Prochaska and Carlo DiClemente at the University of Rhode Island, illustrated how those seeking to recover from addiction go through a series of “stages” that allowed them ultimately to transcend their addictions and maintain sobriety. This means that we meet you where you are mentally, physically and spiritually, helping you to overcome the deep-rooted issues that have caused your addiction to flourish.\nThe staff of Cliffside Malibu was personally trained by Prochaska on this powerful treatment model. It is the hallmark of our treatment program. Learn more about the Stages of Change.\n\n\t\t\n\t\n\n\t\n\t\t\n\t\t\tTHE BEST CALL YOU WILL EVER MAKE\r\nCall now to speak confidentially with an admission counselor.\r\n\r\n(424) 320-3061"}</script><!-- / Schema optimized by Schema Pro --><!-- Schema optimized by Schema Pro --><!-- / Schema optimized by Schema Pro --><!-- breadcrumb Schema optimized by Schema Pro --><script type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"https:\/\/schema.org","@type":"BreadcrumbList","itemListElement":[{"@type":"ListItem","position":1,"item":{"@id":"https:\/\/www.cliffsidemalibu.com\/","name":"Home"}},{"@type":"ListItem","position":2,"item":{"@id":"https:\/\/www.cliffsidemalibu.com\/treatment-program\/","name":"Treatment Programs"}},{"@type":"ListItem","position":3,"item":{"@id":"https:\/\/www.cliffsidemalibu.com\/treatment-program\/residential-rehab\/","name":"Luxury Residential Treatment"}}]}</script><!-- / breadcrumb Schema optimized by Schema Pro -->

				<div class="container">
				    <div class="row">
				    
				        <div class="col-sm-12 text-center">
				            <h1 class="uppercase mb0">
				            	 Luxury Residential Treatment
				            </h3>
				        </div>
				    </div>
				</div>
				<ol class="breadcrumb breadcrumb-2"><li><a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/" class="home-link" rel="home">Home</a></li><li><a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/treatment-program/">Treatment Programs</a></li><li class="active">Luxury Residential Treatment</li></ol>
			</section>
		
<div class="ebor-page-wrapper">
	<a id="home" class="in-page-link" href="#"></a>
	<div class="row light-wrapper  light-wrapper pt0 pb0   light-wrapper pt0 pb0 "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<section class="image-square left">
			    <div class="col-md-6 image">
			        <div class="background-image-holder">
			            <img decoding="async" width="876" height="602" src="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/Massage-WO-e1578681085701.jpg" class="background-image" alt="Find Help Today Test" srcset="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/Massage-WO-e1578681085701.jpg 876w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/Massage-WO-e1578681085701-600x412.jpg 600w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/Massage-WO-e1578681085701-300x206.jpg 300w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/Massage-WO-e1578681085701-768x528.jpg 768w" sizes="(max-width: 876px) 100vw, 876px" />
			        </div>
			    </div>
			    <div class="col-md-6 col-md-offset-1 content">
			        <p style="text-align: left;font-family:Raleway;font-weight:400;font-style:normal" class="vc_custom_heading" >If you are resisting the idea of entering residential rehab, it may be because you do not realize that you have an alcohol or drug problem. There are a few simple tests that you can use to determine if you do. Ask yourself these questions:</p>
<p>Do you feel that you need a drink or a pill before facing certain situations, or even before starting the day? Does your life feel incomplete without alcohol or a drug? Have your family relationships suffered because of your drinking or your dependence on a pill?</p>
<p><strong>If you said yes to any of those questions, then you are addicted.</strong> It is rarely a good idea to try to recover on your own. Your chances of gaining lasting sobriety without professional assistance are minimal. Learn more about our luxury residential rehab program.</p>
			    </div>
			</section>
		</div></div></div></div><section class="  light-wrapper pt64 pb64  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper pt64 pb64 vc_custom_1580844923728"><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper">
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p><a href="https://discoveryaddictionservices.com/locations/cliffside-malibu/levels-of-care/">Cliffside Malibu</a> offers private luxury residential rehab in California through our many residential and outpatient locations. We are located in Malibu, California, on two acres of privately owned land. You will not have to worry about your confidentiality in our residential rehab; we will respect it in every way. We also offer you the best counselors and therapists that can be found. A few of them are especially able to understand what you are going through because they have been where you are – fighting a <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/treatment-directory/drug-addiction/">drug addiction</a> of some sort. Seeing their success can help you become more determined to win your own battle.</p>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div></div></div></section><section class="  light-wrapper pt64 pb64  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper pt64 pb64 "><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><div class="vc_row wpb_row vc_inner vc_row-fluid"><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-6"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><h2 style="text-align: left" class="vc_custom_heading sub-header" >Personal Help</h2>
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>You do not need to enter a <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/treatment-directory/residential-addiction-treatment-program/">residential rehab</a> that will treat you as just a number or a medical case to be solved. At Cliffside Malibu, you can be assured that this will not happen to you! We care about each person that enters our drug rehab center, and it makes a difference.</p>
<p>Because you have a personal battle to fight against <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/treatment-directory/drug-addiction/">drug addiction</a> or <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/conditions/alcohol/">alcoholism</a>, we will not try to force you into a pre-existing treatment plan. The point of residential rehab is not to rush you through a program, but to help you find your own way to achieve and maintain sobriety. For that reason, our trained counselors and therapists are eager to work with you to devise a personal treatment plan.</p>
<p>Every residential rehab plan needs to be tailored to the individual. We are one of the few rehab centers that do this at all levels of treatment. You can find therapy techniques from both the Western and Eastern hemispheres. We offer the services of an acupuncturist, an equine therapist, and opportunities for individual, family, and group therapies. Give us the chance to help you find your way to sobriety.</p>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-6"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><h2 style="text-align: left" class="vc_custom_heading sub-header" >Alcohol Treatment in Malibu, California</h2>
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>If you are addicted to alcohol, you need to enter a residential <a title="Alcohol Treatment" href="/treatment-directory/alcohol-treatment/">alcohol treatment</a> program. It’s that simple; most people are unable to defeat this addiction without professional help. Seeking help does not indicate that you are a weak person, or that you lack willpower. It says that you are strong enough to admit that you have a problem and that you are committed to beating that problem.</p>
<p>Our luxury residential rehab for alcoholism includes medically-assisted detox by our on-staff medical team, individual, group, and family therapy programs. These group functions can help you see that you are not alone in your struggle. Every client here can contribute something valuable and can possibly encourage somebody else who has not yet reached the same level of success. Give yourself the chance to succeed.</p>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></section><section class="image-bg overlay parallax  light-wrapper pt180 pb180 pt-xs-80 pb-xs-80  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper pt180 pb180 pt-xs-80 pb-xs-80 vc_custom_1580845865164">
			<div class="background-image-holder">
			    <img width="1600" height="1067" decoding="async" alt="Background" class="background-image" src="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-ranch-house-dining-room.jpg?id=24957" />
			</div>
		<div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"></div></div></div></div></div></section><section class="  light-wrapper pt64 pb64  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper pt64 pb64 "><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><div class="vc_row wpb_row vc_inner vc_row-fluid"><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-4"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><h2 style="text-align: left" class="vc_custom_heading sub-header" >Drug Treatment</h2>
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>In our luxury residential rehab in California for drug addiction, you will take place in all three types of therapy: group, individual, and family. Each one of these therapies will yield great benefits, but family therapy is certainly one of the most important aspects of treatment here.</p>
<p>At Cliffside Malibu, we also offer <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/treatment-program/detox/">detoxification</a> help. Do not trust any residential rehab that encourages you to detox outside a facility. The withdrawal symptoms of some drugs can be dangerous and even deadly. You will need professional help to make your detox as painless as possible.</p>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-4"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><h2 style="text-align: left" class="vc_custom_heading sub-header" >Luxury Residential Rehab in California</h2>
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>Whether you are addicted to alcohol or drugs, you deserve to be treated well during your time of recovery. At Cliffside Malibu, we are happy to give you the pampering you want in order to make your experience pleasant. At our residential rehab, you will stay in comfort unparalleled by other drug abuse treatment centers.</p>
<p>From our private bedrooms and bathrooms to the sundecks and gardens, you will be sure to notice the luxury we offer. If you need a massage, we offer the services of a skilled masseuse. All laundry and linen services are provided.</p>
<p>During your stay with us, you will never lack for good food. Our gourmet chef prepares healthy meals to help your body recover. Don’t confuse healthy with tasteless, however! Our dinners are always delicious, and you can even request that your meals be made according to special diet requirements. This is what we think of as luxury residential rehab. Get your treatment underway and become sober again.</p>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-4"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><h2 style="text-align: left" class="vc_custom_heading sub-header" >Family Help</h2>
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>You can’t beat addiction alone. Even residential rehab, by itself, is not a guaranteed “cure” for addiction. If you are going to succeed, you need a support system behind you. We offer continuing care programs to give you that extra bit of help even after you leave our residential rehab.</p>
<p>If family ties have been strained to the point where they are practically nonexistent, you need to work to restore those relationships during residential rehab. Addiction can make you do things you would never otherwise do – and your family will understand that. Our family therapy program can help you and your family to work toward reconciliation. These meetings will be overseen by a trained therapist who can ensure a productive environment. Do not be afraid to admit your addiction or to ask your family for help.</p>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></section><section class="  light-wrapper pt64 pb64  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper pt64 pb64 vc_custom_1580844923728"><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><h2 style="text-align: left" class="vc_custom_heading sub-header" >Stages of Change for Addiction Recovery</h2>
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<div data-ogsb="white">At Cliffside Malibu, we base our individualized luxury residential treatment plans on the Stages of Change model. The Stages of Change model, created by James O. Prochaska and Carlo DiClemente at the University of Rhode Island, illustrated how those seeking to recover from addiction go through a series of “stages” that allowed them ultimately to transcend their addictions and maintain sobriety. This means that we meet you where you are mentally, physically and spiritually, helping you to overcome the deep-rooted issues that have caused your addiction to flourish.<br aria-hidden="true" /><br aria-hidden="true" /></div>
<div data-ogsb="white">The <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/about/our-team/">staff of Cliffside Malibu</a> was personally trained by Prochaska on this powerful treatment model. It is the hallmark of our treatment program. Learn more about the <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/treatment-program/stages-of-change/">Stages of Change</a>.</div>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div></div></div></section><a id="contact" class="in-page-link" href="#"></a><section class="  light-wrapper normal-padding  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper normal-padding "><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper">
	<div class="wpb_raw_code wpb_content_element wpb_raw_html" >

			<h4 style="font-size: 20px; color: #93988c; line-height: 1; text-align: center; font-family: Tenor Sans; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal;">THE BEST CALL YOU WILL EVER MAKE</h4>

    function setCookie(name, value, days) {
        var date = new Date();
        date.setTime(date.getTime() + (days * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000));
        var expires = "; expires=" + date.toGMTString();
        document.cookie = name + "=" + value + expires + ";path=/";
    }
 
    function getParam(p) {
        var match = RegExp('[?&]' + p + '=([^&]*)').exec(window.location.search);
        return match && decodeURIComponent(match[1].replace(/\+/g, ' '));
    }
    var gclid = getParam('gclid');

        var n = name + "=";
        var cookie = document.cookie.split(';');
        for (var i = 0; i < cookie.length; i++) {
            var c = cookie[i];
            while (c.charAt(0) == ' ') {
                c = c.substring(1, c.length);
            }
            if (c.indexOf(n) == 0) {
                return c.substring(n.length, c.length);
            }
        }
        return null;
    }

        document.getElementById('gclid').value = readCookie('gclid');

Luxury Residential Rehab in California – Cliffside Malibu 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 
 CALL FOR A CONFIDENTIAL CONSULTATION 
 Call 
 424.320.3061 
 
 

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 
 

 
 

 
 Treatment Programs 
 
 
 Levels of Care 
 
 
 Detox 

 
 Residential Treatment Program 

 

 
 The Stages of Change 

 
 Treatment Modalities 
 
 
 Somatic Experiencing and EMDR 

 
 Dialectical Behavior Therapy 

 
 Cognitive Behavioral Therapy 

 
 Motivational Interviewing 

 
 Family Systems Therapy 

 

 
 Experiential & Holistic Therapy 
 
 
 Acupuncture 

 
 Massage 

 
 Mindsight & Interpersonal Neurobiology 

 
 Relapse Prevention Therapy 

 
 Yoga Therapy 

 

 
 Treatment Directory 

 

 Your Experience 
 
 
 Why Choose Cliffside Malibu 

 
 Life at Cliffside Malibu 

 
 Life After Cliffside Malibu 

 

 About 
 
 
 Our Approach 

 
 Our Team 

 
 Locations 

 
 Tour 

 
 Brochure 

 
 What We Treat 

 
 Blog 

 
 State Licensing 

 

 Insurance 

 Contact 
 
 
 Admissions 

 

 Careers 

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 Luxury Residential Treatment
 

 

 

 

 
 Home 

 Treatment Programs 

Luxury Residential Treatment
 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
If you are resisting the idea of entering residential rehab, it may be because you do not realize that you have an alcohol or drug problem. There are a few simple tests that you can use to determine if you do. Ask yourself these questions:

Do you feel that you need a drink or a pill before facing certain situations, or even before starting the day? Does your life feel incomplete without alcohol or a drug? Have your family relationships suffered because of your drinking or your dependence on a pill?

 If you said yes to any of those questions, then you are addicted. It is rarely a good idea to try to recover on your own. Your chances of gaining lasting sobriety without professional assistance are minimal. Learn more about our luxury residential rehab program.

 

 

 

 

 

 
 Cliffside Malibu offers private luxury residential rehab in California through our many residential and outpatient locations. We are located in Malibu, California, on two acres of privately owned land. You will not have to worry about your confidentiality in our residential rehab; we will respect it in every way. We also offer you the best counselors and therapists that can be found. A few of them are especially able to understand what you are going through because they have been where you are – fighting a drug addiction of some sort. Seeing their success can help you become more determined to win your own battle.

 

 

Personal Help

 

 

 
You do not need to enter a residential rehab that will treat you as just a number or a medical case to be solved. At Cliffside Malibu, you can be assured that this will not happen to you! We care about each person that enters our drug rehab center, and it makes a difference.

Because you have a personal battle to fight against drug addiction or alcoholism , we will not try to force you into a pre-existing treatment plan. The point of residential rehab is not to rush you through a program, but to help you find your own way to achieve and maintain sobriety. For that reason, our trained counselors and therapists are eager to work with you to devise a personal treatment plan.

Every residential rehab plan needs to be tailored to the individual. We are one of the few rehab centers that do this at all levels of treatment. You can find therapy techniques from both the Western and Eastern hemispheres. We offer the services of an acupuncturist, an equine therapist, and opportunities for individual, family, and group therapies. Give us the chance to help you find your way to sobriety.

 

 

Alcohol Treatment in Malibu, California

 

 

 
If you are addicted to alcohol, you need to enter a residential  alcohol treatment  program. It’s that simple; most people are unable to defeat this addiction without professional help. Seeking help does not indicate that you are a weak person, or that you lack willpower. It says that you are strong enough to admit that you have a problem and that you are committed to beating that problem.

Our luxury residential rehab for alcoholism includes medically-assisted detox by our on-staff medical team, individual, group, and family therapy programs. These group functions can help you see that you are not alone in your struggle. Every client here can contribute something valuable and can possibly encourage somebody else who has not yet reached the same level of success. Give yourself the chance to succeed.

 

 

 

 
 

 

Drug Treatment

 

 

 
In our luxury residential rehab in California for drug addiction, you will take place in all three types of therapy: group, individual, and family. Each one of these therapies will yield great benefits, but family therapy is certainly one of the most important aspects of treatment here.

At Cliffside Malibu, we also offer detoxification help. Do not trust any residential rehab that encourages you to detox outside a facility. The withdrawal symptoms of some drugs can be dangerous and even deadly. You will need professional help to make your detox as painless as possible.

 

 

Luxury Residential Rehab in California

 

 

 
Whether you are addicted to alcohol or drugs, you deserve to be treated well during your time of recovery. At Cliffside Malibu, we are happy to give you the pampering you want in order to make your experience pleasant. At our residential rehab, you will stay in comfort unparalleled by other drug abuse treatment centers.

From our private bedrooms and bathrooms to the sundecks and gardens, you will be sure to notice the luxury we offer. If you need a massage, we offer the services of a skilled masseuse. All laundry and linen services are provided.

During your stay with us, you will never lack for good food. Our gourmet chef prepares healthy meals to help your body recover. Don’t confuse healthy with tasteless, however! Our dinners are always delicious, and you can even request that your meals be made according to special diet requirements. This is what we think of as luxury residential rehab. Get your treatment underway and become sober again.

 

 

Family Help

 

 

 
You can’t beat addiction alone. Even residential rehab, by itself, is not a guaranteed “cure” for addiction. If you are going to succeed, you need a support system behind you. We offer continuing care programs to give you that extra bit of help even after you leave our residential rehab.

If family ties have been strained to the point where they are practically nonexistent, you need to work to restore those relationships during residential rehab. Addiction can make you do things you would never otherwise do – and your family will understand that. Our family therapy program can help you and your family to work toward reconciliation. These meetings will be overseen by a trained therapist who can ensure a productive environment. Do not be afraid to admit your addiction or to ask your family for help.

 

 

Stages of Change for Addiction Recovery

 

 

 
At Cliffside Malibu, we base our individualized luxury residential treatment plans on the Stages of Change model. The Stages of Change model, created by James O. Prochaska and Carlo DiClemente at the University of Rhode Island, illustrated how those seeking to recover from addiction go through a series of “stages” that allowed them ultimately to transcend their addictions and maintain sobriety. This means that we meet you where you are mentally, physically and spiritually, helping you to overcome the deep-rooted issues that have caused your addiction to flourish.

The staff of Cliffside Malibu was personally trained by Prochaska on this powerful treatment model. It is the hallmark of our treatment program. Learn more about the Stages of Change .

 

 

 

 

 

 
THE BEST CALL YOU WILL EVER MAKE

Call now to speak confidentially with an admission counselor.

 (424) 320-3061 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 Drug and Alcohol Treatment Center 
Admissions Office (424) 320-3061 24hrs

Follow Us
 
 
 

 
 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 

 

 

Company Information
 
 
 About Us 

 Careers 

 News Press 

 Contact Us 

 Patient Portal 

 

Select Language
 

Useful Information
 
 
 FAQs 

 Insurance 

 Treatment Programs 

 Locations 

 

Search
 
 
 

Highest Treatment Standard
 

 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 Licensed by the  State Department of Health Care Services 

If this is a life-threatening emergency, please call 911 or the National Suicide Prevention Lifeline at (800) 273-8255 

© 2025 Cliffside Malibu | Website branding by Evertype Brand Design 
 

 
 

 
 

 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 Top 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 

 

 
 Cliffside Malibu has a fresh look and experience. Visit our new website today.

    BELinkBlockGenerator.IND_LINK_BLOCK_TYPE_IMAGE_TYPE = 3;

    BELinkBlockGenerator.REPLACEMENT_STRATEGY_OVERWRITE = 0;
    BELinkBlockGenerator.REPLACEMENT_STRATEGY_POST_APPEND_ELEMENT = 1;
    BELinkBlockGenerator.REPLACEMENT_STRATEGY_PRE_APPEND_ELEMENT = 2;
    BELinkBlockGenerator.REPLACEMENT_STRATEGY_PRE_APPEND_PARENT = 3;

    BELinkBlockGenerator.setMaximumHeadlineLength = function(length) {
      BELinkBlockGenerator.MAXIMUM_HEADLINE_LENGTH = length;
    };

    BELinkBlockGenerator.setMaximumDescriptionLength = function(length) {
      BELinkBlockGenerator.MAXIMUM_DESC_LENGTH = length;
    };

    BELinkBlockGenerator.generateIndividualLinks = function(parentElement, linkStructure, link) {
      var link_level_element_tag = linkStructure[0];
      var link_level_element = document.createElement(link_level_element_tag);
      var link_attribute_dictionary = linkStructure[1];
      var allowable_elements = linkStructure[2];
      var children_link_structures = linkStructure[3];
      for (var link_attribute_key in link_attribute_dictionary) {
        link_level_element.setAttribute(link_attribute_key, link_attribute_dictionary[link_attribute_key]);
      }

      var added_something = false;
      if (allowable_elements.indexOf(BELinkBlockGenerator.IND_LINK_BLOCK_TYPE_URL_TYPE)>=0) {
        link_level_element.setAttribute('href', link.url);

          headline_text = headline_text.substring(0, BELinkBlockGenerator.MAXIMUM_HEADLINE_LENGTH) + '...';
        }
        var text_node = document.createTextNode(headline_text);
        link_level_element.appendChild(text_node);
        added_something = true;
      }
      if (allowable_elements.indexOf(BELinkBlockGenerator.IND_LINK_BLOCK_TYPE_DESCRIPTION_TYPE)>=0 && link.desc) {
        var desc_text = link.desc;
        if (desc_text.length > BELinkBlockGenerator.MAXIMUM_DESC_LENGTH) {

    except for parent structures where allowable_length=0
    */

      }

      /* last level place links */
      if (i == overallStructure.length-1) {
        for (var link_i=0; link_i < links.length; link_i++) {
          var link = links[link_i];
          for (var linkStructureIndex=0;linkStructureIndex < linkStructure.length; linkStructureIndex++) {
            BELinkBlockGenerator.generateIndividualLinks(level_element, linkStructure[linkStructureIndex], link)
          }
        }
      }

      /* first level child we need to check placement */
      if (previousElement == targetElement) {
        if (replacementStrategy == BELinkBlockGenerator.REPLACEMENT_STRATEGY_PRE_APPEND_ELEMENT) {
          /* 2 means insert right before */
          previousElement.insertBefore(level_element, targetElement.firstChild);
        } else if (replacementStrategy == BELinkBlockGenerator.REPLACEMENT_STRATEGY_PRE_APPEND_PARENT) {
          /* 3 means insert right before at parent level */
          var parentElement = previousElement.parentElement;
          parentElement.insertBefore(level_element, previousElement);
        } else {
          previousElement.appendChild(level_element);
        }
      } else {
        previousElement.appendChild(level_element);
      }
      previousElement = level_element;
    }
  };
}(window.BELinkBlockGenerator = window.BELinkBlockGenerator || {}))
};
</script>

/* Add padding to create space for the caret on dropdown menu items on homepage */
.home .has-dropdown {
    padding-right: 28px; /* Add enough space for the caret to avoid overlap */
}

/* Adjust caret color and size, keeping the initial perfect positioning */
.home .has-dropdown:after {
    position: absolute;
    top: 0; /* Keep original vertical positioning */
    right: 10px; /* Maintain original right position */
    font-size: 11px; /* Adjust size to match your preference */
    content: "\e64b"; /* Use the Themify caret icon */
    font-family: 'themify'; /* Ensure caret uses the Themify icon font */
    color: #666666 !important; /* Change the caret color to dark */
    font-weight: normal !important; /* Ensure caret is thin */
}

/* Remove any extra custom arrows from homepage non-sticky menu */
.home .menu-item-has-children > a::after {
    content: none !important; /* Remove any extra arrows */
}


/* Ensure no extra arrows are present in the sticky or submenus */
nav.fixed .menu-item-has-children > a::after,
.menu-item-has-children > ul > li.menu-item-has-children > a::after {
    content: none !important; /* Prevent extra arrows in sticky or submenu items */
}


/* DB: Styling fix for menu-item with current ancestor/parent state */
.menu-item.menu-item-type-custom.current-menu-ancestor.current-menu-parent {
    line-height: 20px;
    margin-top: 0px !important; /* Ensure consistent margin for current ancestor/parent */
}

/* DB: Combined styling fix for specific menu items (menu-item-25441, menu-item-25443, and menu-item-25444) */
li#menu-item-25441.menu-item,
li#menu-item-25443.menu-item.menu-item-type-custom.current-menu-ancestor.current-menu-parent,
li#menu-item-25444.menu-item {
    line-height: 20px;
    margin-top: 45px !important; /* Add 45px top margin */
}

/* DB: Adjust spacing for mobile and tablet (max-width: 991px) */
@media (max-width: 991px) {
    li#menu-item-25441.menu-item,
    li#menu-item-25443.menu-item.menu-item-type-custom.current-menu-ancestor.current-menu-parent,
    li#menu-item-25444.menu-item {
        margin-top: 0px !important; /* Remove extra margin on mobile/tablet */
        line-height: normal !important; /* Set normal line height */
        padding-top: 0px !important; /* Ensure consistent top padding */
        padding-bottom: 0px !important; /* Ensure consistent bottom padding */
    }
}

/* DB: Ensure submenus remain within viewport on tablets and smaller screens (max-width: 1024px) */
@media (max-width: 1024px) {
    .menu > li > ul > li > ul {
        left: auto !important; /* Prevent submenus from going off the screen to the left */
        right: 0 !important; /* Align submenus with the right edge */
        top: 0 !important; /* Align submenu to the top of the parent */
        position: absolute !important; /* Ensure proper positioning */
    }

    /* DB: Make sure parent items with submenus remain clickable */
    .menu > li > ul > li > a {
        pointer-events: auto !important; /* Enable pointer events for clickable parent items */
    }

    /* DB: Remove custom arrow in submenu, retain default arrow */
    .menu > li > ul > li:has(ul) > a::after {
        content: none !important; /* Remove custom arrow */
    }
}

/* DB: Mobile-specific adjustments for better touch experience (max-width: 767px) */
@media (max-width: 767px) {
    .menu > li > ul > li > ul {
        position: static !important; /* Stack submenus directly under parent menu */
        width: 100% !important; /* Make submenu full width */
        right: auto !important; /* Remove right alignment */
        left: 0 !important; /* Align submenu to the left */
        margin-left: -20px !important; /* Prevent submenu from overlapping */
    }

    /* DB: Ensure parent menu items are fully clickable on mobile */
    .menu > li > ul > li > a {
        display: block;
        width: 100%; /* Make parent items take full width */
        padding: 10px 0; /* Add padding for better touch experience */
    }

    /* DB: Position default arrow closer to text in last-level dropdown */
    .menu > li > ul > li > ul > li:has(ul) > a::after {
        content: "\276F"; /* Default arrow */
        font-size: 1.2em;
        margin-left: 6px; /* Position arrow closer to text */
        display: inline-block;
        vertical-align: middle; /* Align arrow with text */
        transform: rotate(90deg); /* Rotate arrow to point downwards */
    }
}
/* DB: End styling fix */
/* DB: Ensure the top-level parent (menu-item-25441) always has a gray caret */
@media (max-width: 991px) {
    /* Target the top-level parent with the ID of menu-item-25441 */
    #menu-item-25441.has-dropdown:after {
        color: #808080!important; /* Gray caret for this top-level parent */
    }

    /* Maintain white caret for dropdowns inside the submenu (for nested dropdowns) */
    body.home nav.transparent .has-dropdown .has-dropdown:after {
        color: #ffffff!important; /* White caret for nested dropdown items */
    }
}
/* DB: White caret for all dropdowns (except uppermost parent) on homepage (desktop and sticky menu) */
@media (min-width: 992px) {
    /* White caret for child dropdowns on homepage (excluding uppermost parent) */
    body.home nav.transparent .has-dropdown .has-dropdown:after {
        color: #ffffff!important; /* White caret for all nested dropdowns */
    }
}

/* DB: Sticky menu fix for white caret (excluding uppermost parent) on homepage */
nav.sticky-menu {
    /* White caret for child dropdowns in sticky menu (excluding uppermost parent) */
    body.home nav.sticky-menu .has-dropdown .has-dropdown:after {
        color: #ffffff!important; /* White caret for all nested dropdowns in sticky menu */
    }

}
h4.mb0:empty,
h4.mb0:empty::before,
h4.mb0:empty::after {
    display: none !important;
}
/* Custom styling for <h1> on team pages */
/* Added by DB (Dan Batten) to ensure specific styles for team member names */
body.single-team h1.mb0 {

    font-size: 25px;
    font-weight: 500;
}
/* ==========================================================================
   Dev Notes: Social Icons Mobile Fix, Hover Effects & Clearfix
   --------------------------------------------------------------------------
   1) Re-enable clicks on preview-only anchors
   2) Expand tap targets on touchscreens
   3) Apply hover/active effects on desktop & mobile
   4) Clear any floats so nothing overlaps on small screens
   ========================================================================== */

/* 1) Undo Customizer’s pointer-events:none lock */
.customize-unpreviewable {
  pointer-events: auto !important;
}

/* 2) Improve tap targets by making links inline-block with padding */
ul.list-inline.social-list li a {
  display: inline-block;    /* allows padding/width on the <a> */
  padding: 0px;             /* increase hit area for touch */
  margin: 0 0px;            /* spacing between icons */
  line-height: 1;           /* vertically center the icon */
}

/* 3) Trigger hover/active effects for mouse & touch users */
ul.list-inline.social-list li a:hover i,
ul.list-inline.social-list li a:active i,
ul.list-inline.social-list li a:focus i {
  color: #ffffff;                  /* change icon color on interaction */
  transform: scale(1.1);           /* slightly enlarge icon */
  transition: transform 0.2s ease; /* smooth scaling animation */
}

/* 4) Clear any floated elements that come after the social list */
.social-list-clear {
  clear: both;   /* forces following content below any floats */
  width: 100%;   /* spans full container (optional) */
  height: 0;     /* no visual height—purely a clearfix */
}
/* Desktop styling for main team member names on the team page */
@media (min-width: 1025px) {
    .about-our-team h2.uppercase,
    body.page-id-56 h2.uppercase { /* Replace XXXX with the page ID of /about/our-team/ */
        font-size: 1.5em!important;
        line-height: 1.5em!important;
    }
}
@media (min-width: 1025px) {
  body.home .image-tile .title h2.uppercase {
    font-size: 1.5em;
    line-height: 1.5em;
  }
}
body.postid-50657 .type-post .post-content h1 {
  display: none !important;
}

But make no mistake, the effort is worth it. To get sober is to rediscover the world as you used to know it and yourself as you used to be; it is to remember why you ever believed life was worth living in the first place. And it all starts with detox.\nTo learn more about our California detox program, contact us.\n\n\t\t\n\t\nStages of Change for Addiction Recovery\n\t\n\t\t\n\t\t\tAt Cliffside Malibu, we base our individualized luxury treatment plans on the Stages of Change model. The Stages of Change model, created by James O. Prochaska and Carlo DiClemente at the University of Rhode Island, illustrated how those seeking to recover from addiction go through a series of “stages” that allowed them ultimately to transcend their addictions and maintain sobriety. This means that we meet you where you are mentally, physically and spiritually, helping you to overcome the deep-rooted issues that have caused your addiction to flourish.\nThe staff of Cliffside Malibu was personally trained by Prochaska on this powerful treatment model. It is the hallmark of our treatment program. Learn more about the Stages of Change.\n\n\t\t\n\t\n\n  \n    \n      \n        \n            Life at Cliffside\n        \n      \n      \n        \n            LIFE AT CLIFFSIDE\n            Treatment programs at Cliffside Malibu have been designed to facilitate a full recovery from addiction.\n\n            Learn More\n        \n      \n    \n  \n\n  \n    \n      \n        \n            Life After Cliffside\n        \n      \n      \n        \n            LIFE AFTER CLIFFSIDE\n            The goal of treatment is to create within you the skills and ability to remain clean and sober at home.\n\n            Learn More\n        \n      \n    \n  \n\n  \n    \n      \n        \n            Why Cliffside?\n        \n      \n      \n        \n            WHY CLIFFSIDE?\n            The answer is simple. We want you to go to treatment once and recover for good.\n\n            Learn More\n        \n      \n    \n  \n\n  \n    \n      \n        \n            View Our Facility\n        \n      \n      \n        \n            VIEW OUR FACILITY\n            Cliffside Malibu is a beautiful luxury estate, situated on two acres, overlooking Zuma Beach.\n\n            Learn More\n        \n      \n    \n  \n\n\t\n\t\t\n\t\t\t\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\nTHE BEST CALL YOU WILL EVER MAKE\r\nCall now to speak confidentially with an admission counselor.\r\n\r\n(424) 320-3061\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\nFirst Name*\r\n\r\n\r\nLast Name*\r\n\r\n\r\nZip Code*\r\n\r\n\r\nEmail*\r\n\r\n\r\nPhone*\r\n\r\n\r\nWho Are You Inquiring About?MyselfA Loved One\r\nAn Employee or Colleague\r\nA Patient\r\nA Client\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\nHow Did You Hear About Us?*\r\nPlease Choose One\r\nOn The Internet\r\nFrom An Alumni\r\nFrom An Employee\r\nFrom Someone At The Hospital\r\nFrom An Insurance Company\r\nFrom A Printed Article Or Advertisement\r\nFrom a Professional\r\nFrom A Staff Member At My School\r\nOn Television\r\nFrom Another Treatment Facility\r\nFrom A Friend Or Family Member\r\nOther\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\nHow Can We Help You?\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n \r\n *Required\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n  \r\nKeep me updated with important News and Events from Cliffside Malibu\r\nBy clicking submit, I acknowledge that I have read and understand the Privacy PolicyPrivate pay and most insured policies (PPO) accepted.
