<div class="main-container"><section class="page-title page-title-3 image-bg overlay"><div class="background-image-holder"><img width="1600" height="2400" src="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-hazy-oceanspray-beach-cliffs-hero-tall.jpg" class="background-image" alt="Cliffside Malibu - Hazy Beach Cliffs" decoding="async" fetchpriority="high" srcset="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-hazy-oceanspray-beach-cliffs-hero-tall.jpg 1600w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-hazy-oceanspray-beach-cliffs-hero-tall-200x300.jpg 200w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-hazy-oceanspray-beach-cliffs-hero-tall-683x1024.jpg 683w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-hazy-oceanspray-beach-cliffs-hero-tall-768x1152.jpg 768w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-hazy-oceanspray-beach-cliffs-hero-tall-1024x1536.jpg 1024w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-hazy-oceanspray-beach-cliffs-hero-tall-1365x2048.jpg 1365w" sizes="(max-width: 1600px) 100vw, 1600px" /></div>
				<div class="container">
				    <div class="row">
				    
				        <div class="col-sm-12 text-center">
				            <h1 class="uppercase mb0">
				            	 Detox
				            </h3>
				        </div>
				    </div>
				</div>
				<ol class="breadcrumb breadcrumb-2"><li><a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/" class="home-link" rel="home">Home</a></li><li><a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/treatment-program/">Treatment Programs</a></li><li class="active">Detox</li></ol>
			</section>
		
<div class="ebor-page-wrapper">
	<a id="home" class="in-page-link" href="#"></a>
	<section class="  light-wrapper pt64 pb64  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper pt64 pb64 vc_custom_1580315170002"><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><p style="font-size: 17px;line-height: 2;text-align: center;font-family:Cardo;font-weight:400;font-style:italic" class="vc_custom_heading" >Detox is an essential first step on the road to recovery. When you use drugs or alcohol for an extended period of time, dangerous and harmful toxins build up in your system. The detoxification process works to eliminate these toxins and free you from your physiological dependence on alcohol or drugs. Once you are free of the physiological addiction, your body and mind are prepared for the therapeutic work that follows in the next stage of treatment. Contact us today if you want to know more about detox in malibu, ca.</p></div></div></div></div></div></section><section class="  light-wrapper pt64 pb64  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper pt64 pb64 "><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><div class="vc_row wpb_row vc_inner vc_row-fluid"><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-6"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><h2 style="font-size: 20px;color: #003057;line-height: 1.5;text-align: left;font-family:Tenor Sans;font-weight:400;font-style:normal" class="vc_custom_heading sub-header" >A Premium Luxury Detox Program in California</h2>
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>At Cliffside Malibu, located in Malibu, California, we create individual plans for our clients at every stage of treatment, including <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/treatment-directory/drug-detox/">detox</a>. Our detox methods are successful because we put great care and planning into the alleviation of the physical symptoms of your withdrawal. Our programs employ an advanced range of medical therapies, all tailored to particular forms of addiction. Detoxification from <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/alcohol/">alcohol</a>, <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/conditions/cocaine/">cocaine</a>, <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/conditions/heroin/">heroin</a>, and <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/conditions/prescription-drug/">prescription drugs</a> all require specialized medical expertise. An individualized, professionally managed detox program is the foundation for achieving meaningful and lasting sobriety.</p>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-6"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><p style="font-size: 20px;color: #003057;line-height: 1.5;text-align: left;font-family:Tenor Sans;font-weight:400;font-style:normal" class="vc_custom_heading sub-header" >Understanding Your Detox Program</p>
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>Your detox program helps your body recover from the physical dependencies associated with addiction in a safe, supportive environment. Drug addiction works by warping a person with substance use disorder’s metabolism so that he or she literally needs the drugs to sustain normal functioning. Drug and alcohol detox work by gently weaning your body off of the harmful chemicals it was depending on to function and allowing for the restoration of normal metabolic function. Remember, “comfortable detox” doesn’t have to be a contradiction in terms.</p>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></section><div class="row light-wrapper  light-wrapper pt0 pb0   light-wrapper pt0 pb0 vc_custom_1580843221150"><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<section class="image-square left">
			    <div class="col-md-6 image">
			        <div class="background-image-holder">
			            
			        </div>
			    </div>
			    <div class="col-md-6 col-md-offset-1 content">
			        
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p><strong>Here’s what you can expect during detox in Malibu, California</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>A qualified addiction specialist will supervise your detox process.</li>
<li>Your treatment is personalized to meet your needs. Programs differ based on a patient’s overall health, the types of substances used, the duration used, and the amount used.</li>
<li>Comfort during the process is of the highest importance. We insist on proper nutrition, a peaceful setting, and expert staff to help you navigate through the withdrawal process.</li>
</ul>

		</div>
	</div>

			    </div>
			</section>
		</div></div></div></div><section class="  light-wrapper pt64 pb64  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper pt64 pb64 "><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><h2 style="font-size: 20px;color: #003057;line-height: 1.5;text-align: center;font-family:Tenor Sans;font-weight:400;font-style:normal" class="vc_custom_heading sub-header" >Detox And Drug Rehab</h2>
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p style="text-align: center;">It’s important to reiterate that detox is the beginning of the drug rehab process, not the end. A successful detox process is vital to your prospects for long-term recovery, especially since the time you spend in this phase of your recovery is the foundation for everything that follows it. Those who receive only detox support with no other treatment will almost always return to substance abuse, usually in the short term. Cliffside Malibu integrates detox as intimately as possible with our drug rehabilitation programs so that clients can build on the momentum they’ve attained during detox in order to successfully navigate the comprehensive addiction treatment that comes next.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;">Remember, <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/treatment-directory/drug-addiction-programs/">addiction recovery</a> is a process that requires full participation and effort. But make no mistake, the effort is worth it. To get sober is to rediscover the world as you used to know it and yourself as you used to be; it is to remember why you ever believed life was worth living in the first place. And it all starts with detox.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;">To learn more about our California detox program, <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/contact/">contact us</a>.</p>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div></div></div></section><section class="  light-wrapper pt64 pb64  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper pt64 pb64 vc_custom_1580315170002"><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><h2 style="font-size: 20px;color: #003057;line-height: 1.5;text-align: center;font-family:Tenor Sans;font-weight:400;font-style:normal" class="vc_custom_heading sub-header" >Stages of Change for Addiction Recovery</h2>
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<div data-ogsb="white">At Cliffside Malibu, we base our individualized luxury treatment plans on the Stages of Change model. The Stages of Change model, created by James O. Prochaska and Carlo DiClemente at the University of Rhode Island, illustrated how those seeking to recover from addiction go through a series of “stages” that allowed them ultimately to transcend their addictions and maintain sobriety. This means that we meet you where you are mentally, physically and spiritually, helping you to overcome the deep-rooted issues that have caused your addiction to flourish.<br aria-hidden="true" /><br aria-hidden="true" /></div>
<div data-ogsb="white">The <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/about/our-team/">staff of Cliffside Malibu</a> was personally trained by Prochaska on this powerful treatment model. It is the hallmark of our treatment program. Learn more about the <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/treatment-program/stages-of-change/">Stages of Change</a>.</div>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div></div></div></section><div class="row light-wrapper  light-wrapper pt0 pb0   light-wrapper pt0 pb0 "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-6"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><div class="vc-hoverbox-wrapper  sub-header vc-hoverbox-shape--square vc-hoverbox-align--center vc-hoverbox-direction--default vc-hoverbox-width--100"  ontouchstart="">

    function setCookie(name, value, days) {
        var date = new Date();
        date.setTime(date.getTime() + (days * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000));
        var expires = "; expires=" + date.toGMTString();
        document.cookie = name + "=" + value + expires + ";path=/";
    }
 
    function getParam(p) {
        var match = RegExp('[?&]' + p + '=([^&]*)').exec(window.location.search);
        return match && decodeURIComponent(match[1].replace(/\+/g, ' '));

            while (c.charAt(0) == ' ') {

            }
            if (c.indexOf(n) == 0) {
                return c.substring(n.length, c.length);
            }
        }
        return null;
    }

Luxury Detox Program in Malibu, California - Reimagine Recovery 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 
 CALL FOR A CONFIDENTIAL CONSULTATION 
 Call 
 424.320.3061 
 
 

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 
 

 
 

 
 Treatment Programs 
 
 
 Levels of Care 
 
 
 Detox 

 
 Residential Treatment Program 

 

 
 The Stages of Change 

 
 Treatment Modalities 
 
 
 Somatic Experiencing and EMDR 

 
 Dialectical Behavior Therapy 

 
 Cognitive Behavioral Therapy 

 
 Motivational Interviewing 

 
 Family Systems Therapy 

 

 
 Experiential & Holistic Therapy 
 
 
 Acupuncture 

 
 Massage 

 
 Mindsight & Interpersonal Neurobiology 

 
 Relapse Prevention Therapy 

 
 Yoga Therapy 

 

 
 Treatment Directory 

 

 Your Experience 
 
 
 Why Choose Cliffside Malibu 

 
 Life at Cliffside Malibu 

 
 Life After Cliffside Malibu 

 

 About 
 
 
 Our Approach 

 
 Our Team 

 
 Locations 

 
 Tour 

 
 Brochure 

 
 What We Treat 

 
 Blog 

 
 State Licensing 

 

 Insurance 

 Contact 
 
 
 Admissions 

 

 Careers 

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 Detox
 

 

 

 

 
 Home 

 Treatment Programs 

Detox
 
 

 

 
 

Detox is an essential first step on the road to recovery. When you use drugs or alcohol for an extended period of time, dangerous and harmful toxins build up in your system. The detoxification process works to eliminate these toxins and free you from your physiological dependence on alcohol or drugs. Once you are free of the physiological addiction, your body and mind are prepared for the therapeutic work that follows in the next stage of treatment. Contact us today if you want to know more about detox in malibu, ca.

A Premium Luxury Detox Program in California

 

 

 
At Cliffside Malibu, located in Malibu, California, we create individual plans for our clients at every stage of treatment, including detox . Our detox methods are successful because we put great care and planning into the alleviation of the physical symptoms of your withdrawal. Our programs employ an advanced range of medical therapies, all tailored to particular forms of addiction. Detoxification from alcohol , cocaine , heroin , and prescription drugs all require specialized medical expertise. An individualized, professionally managed detox program is the foundation for achieving meaningful and lasting sobriety.

 

 

Understanding Your Detox Program

 

 

 
Your detox program helps your body recover from the physical dependencies associated with addiction in a safe, supportive environment. Drug addiction works by warping a person with substance use disorder’s metabolism so that he or she literally needs the drugs to sustain normal functioning. Drug and alcohol detox work by gently weaning your body off of the harmful chemicals it was depending on to function and allowing for the restoration of normal metabolic function. Remember, “comfortable detox” doesn’t have to be a contradiction in terms.

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 Here’s what you can expect during detox in Malibu, California 

 

A qualified addiction specialist will supervise your detox process.

Your treatment is personalized to meet your needs. Programs differ based on a patient’s overall health, the types of substances used, the duration used, and the amount used.

Comfort during the process is of the highest importance. We insist on proper nutrition, a peaceful setting, and expert staff to help you navigate through the withdrawal process.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Detox And Drug Rehab

 

 

 
It’s important to reiterate that detox is the beginning of the drug rehab process, not the end. A successful detox process is vital to your prospects for long-term recovery, especially since the time you spend in this phase of your recovery is the foundation for everything that follows it. Those who receive only detox support with no other treatment will almost always return to substance abuse, usually in the short term. Cliffside Malibu integrates detox as intimately as possible with our drug rehabilitation programs so that clients can build on the momentum they’ve attained during detox in order to successfully navigate the comprehensive addiction treatment that comes next.

Remember, addiction recovery is a process that requires full participation and effort. But make no mistake, the effort is worth it. To get sober is to rediscover the world as you used to know it and yourself as you used to be; it is to remember why you ever believed life was worth living in the first place. And it all starts with detox.

To learn more about our California detox program, contact us .

 

 

Stages of Change for Addiction Recovery

 

 

 
At Cliffside Malibu, we base our individualized luxury treatment plans on the Stages of Change model. The Stages of Change model, created by James O. Prochaska and Carlo DiClemente at the University of Rhode Island, illustrated how those seeking to recover from addiction go through a series of “stages” that allowed them ultimately to transcend their addictions and maintain sobriety. This means that we meet you where you are mentally, physically and spiritually, helping you to overcome the deep-rooted issues that have caused your addiction to flourish.

The staff of Cliffside Malibu was personally trained by Prochaska on this powerful treatment model. It is the hallmark of our treatment program. Learn more about the Stages of Change .

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Life at Cliffside

 

 

 

 

 
LIFE AT CLIFFSIDE

 
Treatment programs at Cliffside Malibu have been designed to facilitate a full recovery from addiction.

 
 Learn More 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Life After Cliffside

 

 

 

 

 
LIFE AFTER CLIFFSIDE

 
The goal of treatment is to create within you the skills and ability to remain clean and sober at home.

 
 Learn More 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Why Cliffside?

 

 

 

 

 
WHY CLIFFSIDE?

 
The answer is simple. We want you to go to treatment once and recover for good.

 
 Learn More 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
View Our Facility

 

 

 

 

 
VIEW OUR FACILITY

 
Cliffside Malibu is a beautiful luxury estate, situated on two acres, overlooking Zuma Beach.

 
 Learn More 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
THE BEST CALL YOU WILL EVER MAKE

Call now to speak confidentially with an admission counselor.

 (424) 320-3061 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 Drug and Alcohol Treatment Center 
Admissions Office (424) 320-3061 24hrs

      var link_level_element_tag = linkStructure[0];
      var link_level_element = document.createElement(link_level_element_tag);
      var link_attribute_dictionary = linkStructure[1];
      var allowable_elements = linkStructure[2];

      for (var link_attribute_key in link_attribute_dictionary) {

      if (allowable_elements.indexOf(BELinkBlockGenerator.IND_LINK_BLOCK_TYPE_URL_TYPE)>=0) {

      if (allowable_elements.indexOf(BELinkBlockGenerator.IND_LINK_BLOCK_TYPE_DESCRIPTION_TYPE)>=0 && link.desc) {
        var desc_text = link.desc;
        if (desc_text.length > BELinkBlockGenerator.MAXIMUM_DESC_LENGTH) {

}

#gtranslate_wrapper .select-option {

}

/*WP Form Styling*/

}
.wpforms-submit {

}
.single-post .col-md-3 a.cta-phone-number {

}

}
.footer-disclaimer {

}

footer {

        float: left;
        margin-top: -400px;
        margin-left: -20px;
    }
}

/* Add padding to create space for the caret on dropdown menu items on homepage */
.home .has-dropdown {
    padding-right: 28px; /* Add enough space for the caret to avoid overlap */
}

/* Adjust caret color and size, keeping the initial perfect positioning */
.home .has-dropdown:after {
    position: absolute;
    top: 0; /* Keep original vertical positioning */
    right: 10px; /* Maintain original right position */
    font-size: 11px; /* Adjust size to match your preference */
    content: "\e64b"; /* Use the Themify caret icon */
    font-family: 'themify'; /* Ensure caret uses the Themify icon font */
    color: #666666 !important; /* Change the caret color to dark */
    font-weight: normal !important; /* Ensure caret is thin */
}

/* Remove any extra custom arrows from homepage non-sticky menu */
.home .menu-item-has-children > a::after {
    content: none !important; /* Remove any extra arrows */
}


/* Ensure no extra arrows are present in the sticky or submenus */
nav.fixed .menu-item-has-children > a::after,
.menu-item-has-children > ul > li.menu-item-has-children > a::after {
    content: none !important; /* Prevent extra arrows in sticky or submenu items */
}


/* DB: Styling fix for menu-item with current ancestor/parent state */
.menu-item.menu-item-type-custom.current-menu-ancestor.current-menu-parent {
    line-height: 20px;
    margin-top: 0px !important; /* Ensure consistent margin for current ancestor/parent */
}

/* DB: Combined styling fix for specific menu items (menu-item-25441, menu-item-25443, and menu-item-25444) */
li#menu-item-25441.menu-item,
li#menu-item-25443.menu-item.menu-item-type-custom.current-menu-ancestor.current-menu-parent,
li#menu-item-25444.menu-item {
    line-height: 20px;
    margin-top: 45px !important; /* Add 45px top margin */
}

/* DB: Adjust spacing for mobile and tablet (max-width: 991px) */
@media (max-width: 991px) {
    li#menu-item-25441.menu-item,
    li#menu-item-25443.menu-item.menu-item-type-custom.current-menu-ancestor.current-menu-parent,
    li#menu-item-25444.menu-item {
        margin-top: 0px !important; /* Remove extra margin on mobile/tablet */
        line-height: normal !important; /* Set normal line height */
        padding-top: 0px !important; /* Ensure consistent top padding */
        padding-bottom: 0px !important; /* Ensure consistent bottom padding */
    }
}

/* DB: Ensure submenus remain within viewport on tablets and smaller screens (max-width: 1024px) */
@media (max-width: 1024px) {
    .menu > li > ul > li > ul {
        left: auto !important; /* Prevent submenus from going off the screen to the left */
        right: 0 !important; /* Align submenus with the right edge */
        top: 0 !important; /* Align submenu to the top of the parent */
        position: absolute !important; /* Ensure proper positioning */
    }

    /* DB: Make sure parent items with submenus remain clickable */
    .menu > li > ul > li > a {
        pointer-events: auto !important; /* Enable pointer events for clickable parent items */
    }

    /* DB: Remove custom arrow in submenu, retain default arrow */
    .menu > li > ul > li:has(ul) > a::after {
        content: none !important; /* Remove custom arrow */
    }
}

/* DB: Mobile-specific adjustments for better touch experience (max-width: 767px) */
@media (max-width: 767px) {
    .menu > li > ul > li > ul {
        position: static !important; /* Stack submenus directly under parent menu */
        width: 100% !important; /* Make submenu full width */
        right: auto !important; /* Remove right alignment */
        left: 0 !important; /* Align submenu to the left */
        margin-left: -20px !important; /* Prevent submenu from overlapping */
    }

    /* DB: Ensure parent menu items are fully clickable on mobile */
    .menu > li > ul > li > a {
        display: block;
        width: 100%; /* Make parent items take full width */
        padding: 10px 0; /* Add padding for better touch experience */
    }

    /* DB: Position default arrow closer to text in last-level dropdown */
    .menu > li > ul > li > ul > li:has(ul) > a::after {
        content: "\276F"; /* Default arrow */
        font-size: 1.2em;
        margin-left: 6px; /* Position arrow closer to text */
        display: inline-block;
        vertical-align: middle; /* Align arrow with text */
        transform: rotate(90deg); /* Rotate arrow to point downwards */
    }
}
/* DB: End styling fix */
/* DB: Ensure the top-level parent (menu-item-25441) always has a gray caret */
@media (max-width: 991px) {
    /* Target the top-level parent with the ID of menu-item-25441 */
    #menu-item-25441.has-dropdown:after {
        color: #808080!important; /* Gray caret for this top-level parent */
    }

    /* Maintain white caret for dropdowns inside the submenu (for nested dropdowns) */
    body.home nav.transparent .has-dropdown .has-dropdown:after {
        color: #ffffff!important; /* White caret for nested dropdown items */
    }
}
/* DB: White caret for all dropdowns (except uppermost parent) on homepage (desktop and sticky menu) */
@media (min-width: 992px) {
    /* White caret for child dropdowns on homepage (excluding uppermost parent) */
    body.home nav.transparent .has-dropdown .has-dropdown:after {
        color: #ffffff!important; /* White caret for all nested dropdowns */
    }
}

/* DB: Sticky menu fix for white caret (excluding uppermost parent) on homepage */
nav.sticky-menu {
    /* White caret for child dropdowns in sticky menu (excluding uppermost parent) */
    body.home nav.sticky-menu .has-dropdown .has-dropdown:after {
        color: #ffffff!important; /* White caret for all nested dropdowns in sticky menu */
    }

}
h4.mb0:empty,
h4.mb0:empty::before,
h4.mb0:empty::after {
    display: none !important;
}
/* Custom styling for <h1> on team pages */
/* Added by DB (Dan Batten) to ensure specific styles for team member names */
body.single-team h1.mb0 {
    margin-bottom: -15px !important;
    font-size: 25px;
    font-weight: 500;
}
/* ==========================================================================
   Dev Notes: Social Icons Mobile Fix, Hover Effects & Clearfix
   --------------------------------------------------------------------------
   1) Re-enable clicks on preview-only anchors
   2) Expand tap targets on touchscreens
   3) Apply hover/active effects on desktop & mobile
   4) Clear any floats so nothing overlaps on small screens
   ========================================================================== */

/* 1) Undo Customizer’s pointer-events:none lock */
.customize-unpreviewable {
  pointer-events: auto !important;
}

/* 2) Improve tap targets by making links inline-block with padding */
ul.list-inline.social-list li a {
  display: inline-block;    /* allows padding/width on the <a> */
  padding: 0px;             /* increase hit area for touch */
  margin: 0 0px;            /* spacing between icons */
  line-height: 1;           /* vertically center the icon */
}

/* 3) Trigger hover/active effects for mouse & touch users */
ul.list-inline.social-list li a:hover i,
ul.list-inline.social-list li a:active i,
ul.list-inline.social-list li a:focus i {
  color: #ffffff;                  /* change icon color on interaction */
  transform: scale(1.1);           /* slightly enlarge icon */
  transition: transform 0.2s ease; /* smooth scaling animation */
}

/* 4) Clear any floated elements that come after the social list */
.social-list-clear {
  clear: both;   /* forces following content below any floats */
  width: 100%;   /* spans full container (optional) */
  height: 0;     /* no visual height—purely a clearfix */
}
/* Desktop styling for main team member names on the team page */
@media (min-width: 1025px) {
    .about-our-team h2.uppercase,
    body.page-id-56 h2.uppercase { /* Replace XXXX with the page ID of /about/our-team/ */
        font-size: 1.5em!important;
        line-height: 1.5em!important;
    }
}
@media (min-width: 1025px) {
  body.home .image-tile .title h2.uppercase {
    font-size: 1.5em;
    line-height: 1.5em;
  }
}
body.postid-50657 .type-post .post-content h1 {
  display: none !important;
}

<div class="main-container"><section class="page-title page-title-3 image-bg overlay"><div class="background-image-holder"><img width="1600" height="2400" src="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/photo-of-people-on-beach-3639605.jpg" class="background-image" alt="Cliffside Malibu beautify beach view during the day" decoding="async" fetchpriority="high" srcset="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/photo-of-people-on-beach-3639605.jpg 1600w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/photo-of-people-on-beach-3639605-200x300.jpg 200w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/photo-of-people-on-beach-3639605-683x1024.jpg 683w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/photo-of-people-on-beach-3639605-768x1152.jpg 768w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/photo-of-people-on-beach-3639605-1024x1536.jpg 1024w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/photo-of-people-on-beach-3639605-1365x2048.jpg 1365w" sizes="(max-width: 1600px) 100vw, 1600px" /></div>
				<div class="container">
				    <div class="row">
				    
				        <div class="col-sm-12 text-center">
				            <h1 class="uppercase mb0">
				            	 Cognitive Behavioral Therapy
				            </h3>
				        </div>
				    </div>
				</div>
				<ol class="breadcrumb breadcrumb-2"><li><a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/" class="home-link" rel="home">Home</a></li><li><a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/treatment-program/">Treatment Programs</a></li><li class="active">Cognitive Behavioral Therapy</li></ol>
			</section>
		
<div class="ebor-page-wrapper">
	<a id="home" class="in-page-link" href="#"></a>
	<section class="  light-wrapper pt64 pb64  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper pt64 pb64 vc_custom_1580315170002"><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper">
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>There’s no question that how we think can have a huge impact on our behavior and mental wellbeing. Sometimes people think in ways that are unhelpful or counterproductive to living well. For example, people may think there are aspects of themselves that they’re unable to change, obstacles they’re incapable of overcoming or elements working against them to keep them from living their best life.</p>
<p>Thoughts like these can have a profound way of influencing one’s behavior and emotions, and even make a person dysfunctional. Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) can help by directly confronting such thoughts and change them for the better.</p>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div></div></div></section><section class="  light-wrapper pt64 pb64  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper pt64 pb64 vc_custom_1581533612275"><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><h2 style="font-size: 20px;color: #003057;line-height: 1.5;text-align: center;font-family:Tenor Sans;font-weight:400;font-style:normal" class="vc_custom_heading sub-header" >What Is CBT?</h2><div class="vc_row wpb_row vc_inner vc_row-fluid"><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-6"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper">
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>CBT is a form of therapy that aims to confront and reconstruct a person’s irrational or negative beliefs. It’s a form of talk therapy, meaning it primarily involves talking through these issues and discussing their solutions. It begins by identifying what negative thoughts a person may have and determining whether they reflect reality or not. If not, there are strategies that can be used to overcome them.</p>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-6"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper">
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>CBT is often described as a results-based form of psychotherapy. It focuses on addressing a person’s thoughts and behavior in the present, rather than other forms of therapy that focus on a person’s past. It was developed in the 1960s by a psychiatrist named Aaron Beck. Since then, it has become a helpful tool for helping people with many issues including <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/treatment-directory/anxiety/">anxiety disorders</a>, depression, eating disorders, severe mental illness and substance abuse.</p>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></section><div class="row light-wrapper  light-wrapper pt0 pb0   light-wrapper pt0 pb0 "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<section class="image-square left">
			    <div class="col-md-6 image">
			        <div class="background-image-holder">
			            <img decoding="async" width="1600" height="1067" src="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/photo-of-ocean-758733.jpg" class="background-image" alt="Ocean view" srcset="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/photo-of-ocean-758733.jpg 1600w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/photo-of-ocean-758733-300x200.jpg 300w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/photo-of-ocean-758733-1024x683.jpg 1024w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/photo-of-ocean-758733-768x512.jpg 768w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/photo-of-ocean-758733-1536x1024.jpg 1536w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/photo-of-ocean-758733-600x400.jpg 600w" sizes="(max-width: 1600px) 100vw, 1600px" />
			        </div>
			    </div>
			    <div class="col-md-6 col-md-offset-1 content">
			        <h2 style="font-size: 20px;color: #003057;line-height: 1.5;text-align: left;font-family:Tenor Sans;font-weight:400;font-style:normal" class="vc_custom_heading sub-header" >How Does CBT Work?</h2>
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>CBT works by confronting one’s negative thoughts and emotions, helping people learn new patterns of behavior. The practice rests on a few basic assumptions about humans in general. It presupposes that psychological problems are based, at least in part, on faulty ways of thinking and unhelpful patterns of learned behavior. It allows people to learn better ways of coping with their problems and ultimately reduce their symptoms.</p>

		</div>
	</div>

			    </div>
			</section>
		</div></div></div></div><div class="row light-wrapper  light-wrapper pt0 pb0   light-wrapper pt0 pb0 "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<section class="image-square right">
			    <div class="col-md-6 image">
			        <div class="background-image-holder">
			            <img decoding="async" width="1600" height="1067" src="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/cliffside-malibu-sunset.jpg" class="background-image" alt="Cliffside Malibu - Sunset" srcset="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/cliffside-malibu-sunset.jpg 1600w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/cliffside-malibu-sunset-300x200.jpg 300w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/cliffside-malibu-sunset-1024x683.jpg 1024w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/cliffside-malibu-sunset-768x512.jpg 768w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/cliffside-malibu-sunset-1536x1024.jpg 1536w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/cliffside-malibu-sunset-600x400.jpg 600w" sizes="(max-width: 1600px) 100vw, 1600px" />
			        </div>
			    </div>
			    <div class="col-md-6 content">
			        
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>To reach that point, there are a variety of strategies the psychiatrist and patient can use, such as:</p>
<ul>
<li>Recognizing what patterns in a person’s thoughts are disrupting their lives and reevaluating them</li>
<li>Learning to develop great confidence in one’s abilities</li>
<li>Learning to better understand the behavior and motivations of others</li>
<li>Using problem-solving skills to find solutions</li>
<li>Learning to calm and relax one’s body</li>
<li>Using role-playing to address potentially problematic interactions</li>
<li>Confronting one’s fears</li>
</ul>

		</div>
	</div>

			    </div>
			</section>
		</div></div></div></div><div class="row light-wrapper  light-wrapper pt0 pb0   light-wrapper pt0 pb0 "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<section class="image-square left">
			    <div class="col-md-6 image">
			        <div class="background-image-holder">
			            <img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="1600" height="1067" src="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-detox.jpg" class="background-image" alt="Cliffside Malibu - Detox" srcset="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-detox.jpg 1600w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-detox-300x200.jpg 300w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-detox-1024x683.jpg 1024w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-detox-768x512.jpg 768w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-detox-1536x1024.jpg 1536w, https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/cliffside-malibu-detox-600x400.jpg 600w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 1600px) 100vw, 1600px" />
			        </div>
			    </div>
			    <div class="col-md-6 col-md-offset-1 content">
			        
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>Deciding which strategies to use largely depends on the individual in question and the specific behaviors they’re trying to change. Typically, the therapist and patient will work together to recognize which patterns of thought and behavior need to be addressed and how to address them.</p>

		</div>
	</div>

			    </div>
			</section>
		</div></div></div></div><section class="  light-wrapper pt64 pb64  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper pt64 pb64 vc_custom_1580315170002"><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><h2 style="font-size: 20px;color: #003057;line-height: 1.5;text-align: left;font-family:Tenor Sans;font-weight:400;font-style:normal" class="vc_custom_heading sub-header" >How CBT Can Help</h2>
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>One of the most significant ways in which CBT can help a person is by confronting thoughts that feed a person’s anxiety, depression or other feelings that lead to harmful behavior. This extends to <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/alcohol/">alcohol abuse</a> and substance abuse, especially for someone whose mental or emotional condition has</p>
<p>exacerbated their addiction. Many people who have the desire to change their addictive habits still have trouble with thoughts and feelings that do not reflect reality. Thoughts that may or may not be directly related to their substance misuse. In other words, using CBT to treat a person’s psychological problems can also have positive results in drug or alcohol addiction recovery.</p>
<p>What’s especially relevant is that CBT applies to those with a <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/treatment-directory/dual-diagnosis/">dual diagnosis</a>, also known as co-occurring disorders, which include some kind of substance abuse. The idea is that substance use disorder is created or made worse in response to other disorders such as anxiety or depression. Because these kinds of issues can cause unrealistic or unfair negative thoughts, they may trigger someone into drinking or using drugs. As a result, it is essential to treat these issues in order to help someone recover from their addiction.</p>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div></div></div></section><section class="  light-wrapper pt64 pb64  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper pt64 pb64 vc_custom_1581533612275"><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper"><h2 style="font-size: 20px;color: #003057;line-height: 1.5;text-align: center;font-family:Tenor Sans;font-weight:400;font-style:normal" class="vc_custom_heading sub-header" >Evidence-Based Therapy</h2><div class="vc_row wpb_row vc_inner vc_row-fluid"><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper">
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<p>CBT is a form of analytic therapy and part of what’s known as the third wave of behavior therapy. These kinds of therapy are distinguished by their focus on mindfulness, acceptance and living in the moment. Other examples include <a href="https://www.cliffsidemalibu.com/treatment-program/dialectical-behavioral-therapy/">dialectical behavior therapy (DBT)</a> and acceptance and commitment therapy (ACT.) These therapies have become increasingly more popular since the end of the last century after accumulating enough scientific study to be recognized as a form of <a href="https://dualdiagnosis.org/treatment-therapies-for-dual-diagnosis-patients/evidence-based-treatment-practices/#:~:text=With%20extensive%20scientific%20research%20to%20back%20it%20up%2C,of%20Cognitive%20Behavioral%20Therapy%20on%20many%20different%20disorders.">evidence-based therapy.</a> This essentially means that thousands of patients have already gone through these treatments, and their results have been thoroughly analyzed. The advantage of evidence-based procedures, like these, is that they offer a more substantial expectation to their participants. In other words, the results speak for themselves.</p>
<p>CBT is just one of several therapy options we offer at Cliffside Malibu. Just as every person is unique, every recovery requires a unique treatment strategy, including a form of therapy that works well for them. If you or someone you know is struggling with substance use disorder, then CBT may be part of the treatment plan to overcome addiction for a lifetime.</p>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></section><a id="contact" class="in-page-link" href="#"></a><section class="  light-wrapper normal-padding  vc_row wpb_row vc_row-fluid light-wrapper normal-padding vc_custom_1583945227561"><div class="container "><div class="row "><div class="wpb_column vc_column_container col-sm-12"><div class="vc_column-inner"><div class="wpb_wrapper">
	<div class="wpb_raw_code wpb_content_element wpb_raw_html" >

</div></div></div></div></div></section>

Private and Premium Cognitive Behvarioal Therapy in Malibu, CA

 424.320.3061

 Cognitive Behavioral Therapy
 

 

 

 

 
 Home 

 Treatment Programs 

Cognitive Behavioral Therapy
 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 
There’s no question that how we think can have a huge impact on our behavior and mental wellbeing. Sometimes people think in ways that are unhelpful or counterproductive to living well. For example, people may think there are aspects of themselves that they’re unable to change, obstacles they’re incapable of overcoming or elements working against them to keep them from living their best life.

Thoughts like these can have a profound way of influencing one’s behavior and emotions, and even make a person dysfunctional. Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) can help by directly confronting such thoughts and change them for the better.

 

 

What Is CBT?

 

 

 
CBT is a form of therapy that aims to confront and reconstruct a person’s irrational or negative beliefs. It’s a form of talk therapy, meaning it primarily involves talking through these issues and discussing their solutions. It begins by identifying what negative thoughts a person may have and determining whether they reflect reality or not. If not, there are strategies that can be used to overcome them.

 

 

 

 

 
CBT is often described as a results-based form of psychotherapy. It focuses on addressing a person’s thoughts and behavior in the present, rather than other forms of therapy that focus on a person’s past. It was developed in the 1960s by a psychiatrist named Aaron Beck. Since then, it has become a helpful tool for helping people with many issues including anxiety disorders , depression, eating disorders, severe mental illness and substance abuse.

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
How Does CBT Work?

 

 

 
CBT works by confronting one’s negative thoughts and emotions, helping people learn new patterns of behavior. The practice rests on a few basic assumptions about humans in general. It presupposes that psychological problems are based, at least in part, on faulty ways of thinking and unhelpful patterns of learned behavior. It allows people to learn better ways of coping with their problems and ultimately reduce their symptoms.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 
To reach that point, there are a variety of strategies the psychiatrist and patient can use, such as:

 

Recognizing what patterns in a person’s thoughts are disrupting their lives and reevaluating them

Learning to develop great confidence in one’s abilities

Learning to better understand the behavior and motivations of others

Using problem-solving skills to find solutions

Learning to calm and relax one’s body

Using role-playing to address potentially problematic interactions

Confronting one’s fears

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 
Deciding which strategies to use largely depends on the individual in question and the specific behaviors they’re trying to change. Typically, the therapist and patient will work together to recognize which patterns of thought and behavior need to be addressed and how to address them.

 

 

 

 

 

How CBT Can Help

 

 

 
One of the most significant ways in which CBT can help a person is by confronting thoughts that feed a person’s anxiety, depression or other feelings that lead to harmful behavior. This extends to alcohol abuse and substance abuse, especially for someone whose mental or emotional condition has

exacerbated their addiction. Many people who have the desire to change their addictive habits still have trouble with thoughts and feelings that do not reflect reality. Thoughts that may or may not be directly related to their substance misuse. In other words, using CBT to treat a person’s psychological problems can also have positive results in drug or alcohol addiction recovery.

What’s especially relevant is that CBT applies to those with a dual diagnosis , also known as co-occurring disorders, which include some kind of substance abuse. The idea is that substance use disorder is created or made worse in response to other disorders such as anxiety or depression. Because these kinds of issues can cause unrealistic or unfair negative thoughts, they may trigger someone into drinking or using drugs. As a result, it is essential to treat these issues in order to help someone recover from their addiction.

 

 

Evidence-Based Therapy

 

 

 
CBT is a form of analytic therapy and part of what’s known as the third wave of behavior therapy. These kinds of therapy are distinguished by their focus on mindfulness, acceptance and living in the moment. Other examples include dialectical behavior therapy (DBT) and acceptance and commitment therapy (ACT.) These therapies have become increasingly more popular since the end of the last century after accumulating enough scientific study to be recognized as a form of evidence-based therapy. This essentially means that thousands of patients have already gone through these treatments, and their results have been thoroughly analyzed. The advantage of evidence-based procedures, like these, is that they offer a more substantial expectation to their participants. In other words, the results speak for themselves.

CBT is just one of several therapy options we offer at Cliffside Malibu. Just as every person is unique, every recovery requires a unique treatment strategy, including a form of therapy that works well for them. If you or someone you know is struggling with substance use disorder, then CBT may be part of the treatment plan to overcome addiction for a lifetime.

rawMentions: Detox
Medically Assisted Detox.
Your detox experience can be made more comfortable through medical oversight and skilled medication intervention.
What kind of detox do you do?
An individualized, professionally managed detox program is the foundation for achieving meaningful and lasting sobriety.
Cliffside Malibu is among California’s best alcohol detox centers.

Our evidence-based approach includes combining medicated- assisted detox (if necessary), intensive one-on-one therapy, group sessions, physical activity, nutritional support and many other effective proven modalities.
It begins the way nearly all substance use treatment programs do with detox followed by a period of psychotherapy and other treatments.
Our luxury residential rehab for alcoholism includes medically-assisted detox by our on-staff medical team, individual, group, and family therapy programs.
Do not trust any residential rehab that encourages you to detox outside a facility.
You will need professional help to make your detox as painless as possible.
Luxury Detox Program in Malibu, California - Reimagine Recovery
Detox is an essential first step on the road to recovery.
Contact us today if you want to know more about detox in malibu, ca.
A Premium Luxury Detox Program in California
At Cliffside Malibu, located in Malibu, California, we create individual plans for our clients at every stage of treatment, including detox .
Our detox methods are successful because we put great care and planning into the alleviation of the physical symptoms of your withdrawal.
Understanding Your Detox Program
Your detox program helps your body recover from the physical dependencies associated with addiction in a safe, supportive environment.
Drug and alcohol detox work by gently weaning your body off of the harmful chemicals it was depending on to function and allowing for the restoration of normal metabolic function.
Remember, “comfortable detox” doesn’t have to be a contradiction in terms.
Here’s what you can expect during detox in Malibu, California
A qualified addiction specialist will supervise your detox process.
Detox And Drug Rehab
It’s important to reiterate that detox is the beginning of the drug rehab process, not the end.
A successful detox process is vital to your prospects for long-term recovery, especially since the time you spend in this phase of your recovery is the foundation for everything that follows it.
Those who receive only detox support with no other treatment will almost always return to substance abuse, usually in the short term.

Cliffside Malibu integrates detox as intimately as possible with our drug rehabilitation programs so that clients can build on the momentum they’ve attained during detox in order to successfully navigate the comprehensive addiction treatment that comes next.
And it all starts with detox.
To learn more about our California detox program, contact us .

rawMentions: Residential Treatment Program
With four residential estates and a menu of luxury amenities to choose from, you can leave your daily stressors behind and focus on creating a life free from addiction.
Luxury Residential Rehab in California – Cliffside Malibu
Luxury Residential Treatment
If you are resisting the idea of entering residential rehab, it may be because you do not realize that you have an alcohol or drug problem.
Learn more about our luxury residential rehab program.
Cliffside Malibu offers private luxury residential rehab in California through our many residential and outpatient locations.
You will not have to worry about your confidentiality in our residential rehab; we will respect it in every way.
You do not need to enter a residential rehab that will treat you as just a number or a medical case to be solved.
The point of residential rehab is not to rush you through a program, but to help you find your own way to achieve and maintain sobriety.
Every residential rehab plan needs to be tailored to the individual.
If you are addicted to alcohol, you need to enter a residential alcohol treatment program.
Our luxury residential rehab for alcoholism includes medically-assisted detox by our on-staff medical team, individual, group, and family therapy programs.
In our luxury residential rehab in California for drug addiction, you will take place in all three types of therapy: group, individual, and family.
Do not trust any residential rehab that encourages you to detox outside a facility.
Luxury Residential Rehab in California
At our residential rehab, you will stay in comfort unparalleled by other drug abuse treatment centers.
This is what we think of as luxury residential rehab.
Even residential rehab, by itself, is not a guaranteed “cure” for addiction.
We offer continuing care programs to give you that extra bit of help even after you leave our residential rehab.
If family ties have been strained to the point where they are practically nonexistent, you need to work to restore those relationships during residential rehab.
At Cliffside Malibu, we base our individualized luxury residential treatment plans on the Stages of Change model.

rawMentions: Do you treat dual diagnosis?
Yes, at Cliffside Malibu we treat patients that require dual diagnosis treatment.
Luxury Co-Occurring Disorder Treatment Program in Malibu, California
Co-Occurring Disorder Treatment
Understanding Co-Occurring Disorder
Understanding Co-Occurring Disorder Treatment
The Co-Occurring Disorder Process at Cliffside Malibu
Quality Co-Occurring Disorder Treatment and Luxury Accommodation in Malibu, California

In addition to our evidence-based and tailored co-occurring disorder treatment programs, Cliffside Malibu prides itself on providing a safe and comfortable space for our patients to escape to during this challenging period in their lives.

It is very often used at Cliffside Malibu to assist those with co-occurring disorders (also called dual diagnosis) to regulate the out-of-control feelings that the addict previously used drugs and alcohol to control.
What’s especially relevant is that CBT applies to those with a dual diagnosis , also known as co-occurring disorders, which include some kind of substance abuse.
